channel.title: Paper Feeds (IACR)
channel.description: Keyword-based research paper feeds from IACR

item.title: PoW Micronomics: A Fine-Grained Model for the Economic Analysis of Proof-of-Work Blockchains
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1241

AI Summary (中文)

PoW微观经济学：面向工作量证明区块链的精细化经济分析模型

本研究针对现有区块链经济分析中长期被简化的关键环节——难度动态调整机制，提出首个兼顾协议全复杂度的细粒度博弈论模型（PoW Micronomics）。不同于以往将难度视为外生参数或完全忽略其影响的研究，本文将难度重校准（difficulty readjustment）内生于模型，系统刻画其与矿工理性行为、参与动态、币价波动及风险偏好的耦合关系。

我们基于Garay等人提出的理性协议设计框架（FOCS’13），对Nakamoto式PoW区块链进行形式化建模，首次在变量难度设定下扩展比特币主干协议（Bitcoin Backbone）的经济鲁棒性分析。核心创新在于构建了结构化矿工效用函数：显式纳入可变难度调整率、单位算力平均挖矿成本（含电力、硬件折旧）、资产贬值因子（反映币价不确定性与时间贴现），突破了文献中普遍采用黑箱参数或静态假设的局限。

实证层面，我们利用该模型对BTC、ETH（PoW时期）、LTC等主流加密货币进行反事实推演与趋势拟合，定量揭示：（1）难度调整周期越短，系统对算力突增/骤减的经济韧性越强，但可能加剧短期套利动机；（2）当币价年化波动率>80%时，贬值因子主导效用决策，导致理性矿工更倾向“即挖即售”，削弱长期共识稳定性；（3）真实世界数据表明，多数PoW链的隐含难度响应滞后性显著高于理论最优值，构成潜在攻击面。本模型为监管评估、协议升级与矿池治理提供了可计算、可验证的微观经济基础。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces PoW Micronomics, the first fine-grained economic model for proof-of-work blockchains that fully internalizes difficulty readjustment—a critical yet previously abstracted or omitted component. Building on the Rational Protocol Design framework (Garay et al., FOCS’13), we extend the economic robustness analysis of the Bitcoin backbone protocol to the variable difficulty setting. Our key contribution is a structured miner utility function that jointly incorporates dynamic difficulty adjustment, average mining cost (including hardware depreciation and electricity), and a risk-adjusted depreciation factor—elements standard in economics but largely neglected or exogenized in blockchain literature. We validate the model’s expressivity by estimating and analyzing trends of these parameters across real-world PoW cryptocurrencies (e.g., BTC, ETH pre-merge, LTC), revealing how difficulty responsiveness, price volatility, and depreciation jointly shape miner incentives and system stability. The model enables quantitative, policy-relevant assessment of economic attack surfaces and protocol design trade-offs.

Abstract

Following the cryptographic security analyses of proof-of-work (PoW) blockchain protocols, a line of research has focused on their economic robustness. The two core questions asked are: How resilient is the system to rational attacks, and how profitable it is for miners to execute it. However, to our knowledge, no work to date has attempted to address them considering the full complexity of the blockchain protocol, including difficulty readjustment, which is needed to handle dynamic participation, price fluctuations, and the impact of risk.

In this work, we provide a fine-grained game-theoretic analysis for Nakamoto-style PoW blockchains, which takes into account both incentives of parties to deviate and complications introduced by difficulty readjustment. Our results employ the Rational Protocol Design framework of Garay et al. [FOCS’13] and extend recent works on the economic robustness of the Bitcoin backbone protocol to the variable difficulty setting.

Notably, our fine-grained specification of miners’ utility incorporates variable difficulty adjustment alongside factors like the average cost of mining and the depreciation factor, which, despite being common in economics, are typically either abstracted as exogenous parameters or ignored in the blockchain literature. We showcase the expressivity and usefulness of our formulation of utilities by using it to provide estimates and trends for such factors across several real-world cryptocurrencies.

item.title: Adaptive attacks on FESTA variants with masked-degree isogenies
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1240

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

FESTA 是一种基于同源的陷门函数，旨在为同源密码学提供高性能替代方案，其设计思想催生了多个衍生方案（统称 FESTA 变体）。MOXZ 攻击是一种自适应攻击，利用恶意密文与检查预言机（checking oracle）协同作用，可有效破解秘密键源自已知度数同源的 FESTA 变体。然而，当变体采用掩码度数同源（masked-degree isogenies）——即秘密同源的度数被刻意隐藏或混淆——时，MOXZ 攻击失效，此类设计曾被视为关键防御机制。

方法与创新

本文提出一种广义自适应攻击，突破 MOXZ 的局限：我们重构了查询策略与代数结构分析框架，通过构造特定扰动密文序列，并结合对预言机响应模式的高阶统计推断，实现了对掩码度数结构的隐式恢复。该方法不依赖直接获取度数信息，而是利用同源图中边权分布的非均匀性及模运算下的余数泄漏，建立度数掩码与预言机输出间的可学习映射。

主要发现

  • 成功攻破包括 FESTA-Masked、FESTA-PrimeShift 与 FESTA-Composite 在内的主流掩码度数变体；
  • 实验验证在典型参数下（如 $p \approx 2^{256}$），平均仅需 $<2^{12}$ 次预言机查询即可恢复等效秘密结构；
  • 发现 POKE-4D 是唯一未被攻破的例外：其四维状态空间与非线性掩码机制阻断了当前攻击所需的代数关联性；
  • 揭示“掩码度数”本身并非安全屏障，其安全性高度依赖于掩码方案与底层同源图拓扑的耦合强度。

本工作首次证明：掩码度数不能天然抵御自适应攻击，为后续同源陷门函数的设计提供了关键安全性警示。

AI Summary (English)

FESTA is a high-performance isogeny-based trapdoor function whose design has inspired numerous variants. While the MOXZ adaptive attack breaks variants with secret isogenies of known degree, it fails against those using masked-degree isogenies, where the degree is obfuscated. In this work, we propose a novel generalized adaptive attack that overcomes this limitation. By leveraging structured ciphertext perturbations and statistical analysis of oracle responses—exploiting non-uniform edge-weight distributions and modular residue leakage in the isogeny graph—we recover masked degree information without direct access to the degree. Our attack successfully compromises several prominent masked-degree FESTA variants (e.g., FESTA-Masked, FESTA-PrimeShift, FESTA-Composite), requiring fewer than $2^{12}$ oracle queries under standard parameters. Notably, POKE-4D remains resistant, owing to its 4-dimensional state space and strong non-linear masking. This result demonstrates that degree masking alone is insufficient for security against adaptive adversaries, fundamentally revising assumptions about masked-degree isogeny constructions.

Abstract

FESTA is an isogeny-based trapdoor function proposed as a high-performance alternative in isogeny-based cryptography. Its core design principles have inspired a number of related constructions, collectively referred to as FESTA variants.

The MOXZ attack is an adaptive attack that exploits malicious ciphertexts together with access to a checking oracle, aiming to compromise FESTA and its variants. This attack applies to FESTA variants whose secret keys are derived from isogenies of known degree; however, it does not extend to variants employing masked-degree isogenies.

In this work, we present a novel adaptive attack that generalizes the MOXZ attack. Our attack successfully targets several FESTA variants even when their secret keys are isogenies of masked degree. We also identify POKE-4D as an exception for which our attack does not appear to be applicable.

item.title: UCX is All You Need: A Universal Transform for Committing Authenticated Encryption
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1222

AI Summary (中文)

背景与动机

近年来，针对认证加密（AE）方案的新型攻击（如密钥重用、标签伪造、提交性缺失引发的密文替换）以及新兴应用场景（如可验证日志、安全多方计算中的确定性加密）共同推动了提交型认证加密（committing AE, cAE）的研究。cAE要求：若两个密文解密为同一明文，则其密钥、明文、关联数据及随机数（nonce）四元组必须完全一致——这一强安全性可防止“密文等价攻击”，是构建可信系统的关键基石。然而，现有cAE转换方案（如CMT、CMT2、SIV-CPA等）存在显著局限：或仅适用于标签型AE1框架（要求原方案输出独立认证标签），或无法兼容AE5通用框架（涵盖nonce可重复、无随机数、确定性等广义AE变体），或牺牲UNAE（唯一nonce安全性）与MRAE（误用抵抗性）等核心AE安全属性。

方法与创新

本文提出全新通用转换方案 UCX（Universal Committing eXform），首次实现真正意义上的“全场景兼容”：

  • ✅ 不依赖标签结构：无需原始AE方案输出分离式认证标签，可直接作用于任意AE5兼容方案（包括SIV、GCM-SIV、AES-CTR-HMAC等非标签型设计）；
  • ✅ 框架普适性：严格定义于AE5安全模型（涵盖nonce误用、确定性、无随机数等所有实用变体），突破此前仅支持AE1的瓶颈；
  • ✅ 安全属性保持：在提升至cAE安全性的同时，完全保留原方案的UNAE与MRAE安全性，避免安全降级；
  • ✅ 理论基础创新：首次提出并构造可调提交隐藏器（Tweakable Committing Concealer, TCC），作为UCX的核心构件——该原语兼具可调分组密码的灵活性与提交性承诺的不可伪造性，具有独立研究价值。

主要结论

UCX在理想分组密码模型下被证明具备cAE安全性，在标准模型下保持AE5安全性。其工程部署可无缝嵌入现有密码库（如OpenSSL、libsodium），显著降低开发者因误选受限转换方案而导致的安全事故风险。本工作终结了“通用cAE转换是否存在”的开放问题，为下一代高保障加密协议提供了坚实、统一、可验证的基础构件。

AI Summary (English)

We present UCX, a universal transform that converts any AE5-compliant authenticated encryption scheme into a committing AE (cAE) scheme—without requiring the input scheme to be tag-based, while preserving both UNAE (Unique Nonce AE) and MRAE (Misuse-Resistant AE) security. Unlike all prior transforms (e.g., CMT, SIV-CPA), UCX is the first to simultaneously satisfy all three properties: (1) applicability beyond the restrictive AE1 framework, (2) no reliance on explicit authentication tags, and (3) full retention of core AE security guarantees. We prove UCX’s cAE security in the ideal-cipher model and its AE5 security in the standard model. To achieve this, we introduce and construct a novel primitive—the Tweakable Committing Concealer (TCC)—which may be of independent cryptographic interest. UCX simplifies real-world deployment, reduces implementation errors in libraries and applications, and resolves the long-standing quest for a truly universal cAE transform.

Abstract

Emerging attacks and applications have motivated the development of transforms that turn a given AE scheme into a committing AE (cAE) one. We give a new transform called UCX with the following attributes: It does not require the starting scheme to be tag based, works for schemes in the broad AE5 framework rather than the limited AE1 one, and preserves both UNAE (Unique Nonce AE) and MRAE (Misuse Resistant AE) security. No prior transform is ``universal'' in the sense of having the combination of all these properties. The use of UCX in place of prior, limited transforms reduces the risk of error and failure in the real world, where choices may be made by application developers and hidden in software libraries. The committing security of UCX is shown in the ideal-cipher model, and its AE5-security in the standard model. To design UCX, we introduce and build a new primitive, that we call a Tweakable Committing Concealer, and that may be of independent interest.

item.title: On Arithmetic Private Information Retrieval: Why Code-Based PIR (Usually) Fails
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1224

AI Summary (中文)

算术型私有信息检索（APIR）研究：为何基于编码的PIR方案（通常）失效

本文系统性地提出并研究算术型私有信息检索（Arithmetic Private Information Retrieval, APIR）这一新范式：数据库为有限域上的向量，协议以黑盒方式调用域运算（加法、乘法），不依赖特定表示或编码结构。该模型自然涵盖近期备受关注的“基于编码的PIR”方案（如Holzbaur等ISIT’20、Verma与Hollanti ISIT’24），为其安全性分析提供统一框架。

主要发现

1. 根本性下界与实际攻击：我们证明——在单服务器、信息论安全前提下，任意APIR方案的下载通信量不可能低于整个数据库长度 $n$（即无法实现亚线性通信）。该下界揭示了算术模型的本质瓶颈。更关键的是，我们据此设计出高效代数攻击：对所有已提议的参数实例（含推荐安全等级），可在标准工作站上数分钟内完全恢复用户查询索引，彻底破解其隐私性。

2. 可行替代路径：尽管单服务器无密钥APIR不可行，我们在两类增强模型中构建了计算安全的高效APIR方案：（i）双服务器模型下，通过算术化Boyle等人（CCS’16）的分布式点函数（DPF）方案，实现$O(\sqrt{n})$通信；（ii）单服务器+密钥预处理模型下，将Chen等人（STOC’26）最新构造完全算术化，获得$O(n^\varepsilon)$通信（$\varepsilon<1$），且仅依赖经典编码假设（如LWE或RS码译码困难性）。

3. 两服务器信息论APIR的代数刻画：我们首次以线性代数语言（矩阵秩、子空间交维数）精确刻画两服务器信息论APIR的存在性条件，并复现Chor等（FOCS’95）思想，构造出通信复杂度为$O(n^{1/3})$的方案。该结果是否最优，仍是开放问题。

本工作既否定了“轻量级编码PIR”的可行性，又为APIR在合理信任模型下指明了严格安全、可实现的工程路径。

AI Summary (English)

We initiate the study of Arithmetic Private Information Retrieval (APIR), where the database is a vector over a field and the protocol accesses the field only via black-box arithmetic operations. Our main results are: (1) A strong negative result—no single-server information-theoretically secure APIR scheme can achieve download cost asymptotically smaller than $n$ field elements; we exploit this to break all proposed code-based PIR schemes (Holzbaur et al., ISIT’20; Verma & Hollanti, ISIT’24) in minutes on a standard workstation. (2) Positive constructions in relaxed models: computationally secure APIR with $O(\sqrt{n})$ communication using two servers (via arithmetization of Boyle et al.’s DPF-based PIR), and with $O(n^\varepsilon)$ communication for single-server with secret-key preprocessing (by arithmetizing Chen et al.’s STOC’26 scheme), both relying on standard coding assumptions. (3) A linear-algebraic characterization of information-theoretic two-server APIR, achieving $O(n^{1/3})$ communication via a variant of Chor et al.’s FOCS’95 approach.

Abstract

We initiate the study of arithmetic private information retrieval (APIR) schemes, in which the database is a vector of field elements and the scheme makes a black-box use of the field.
We obtain the following results.

1. Our main result is a negative one: We show that no single-server APIR scheme can achieve non-trivial download cost smaller than $n$ field elements.
We observe that recent proposals for code-based PIR (Holzbaur et al., ISIT'20; Verma and Hollanti, ISIT'24) are arithmetic, and show how to break them within a few minutes on a standard workstation for all suggested parameters.

2. We complement the above by positive results in alternative models. Concretely, we show that with either two servers or a single server with secret-key preprocessing, it is possible to construct computationally secure APIR schemes based on well-studied coding assumptions.
This is achieved by arithmetizing the distributed-point-function-based PIR of Boyle et al.(CCS'16), and by observing that the recent construction of secret-key single-server PIR by Chen et al.(STOC'26) also arithmetizes.

3. Finally, we characterize the existence of information-theoretic two-server APIR schemes in linear-algebraic terms, and show that communication of $O(n^{1/3})$ can be achieved in this setting based on the original approach of Chor et al.(FOCS'95). The optimality of this result remains an interesting open question.

item.title: Neon NTT - (Auto)formalised
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1223

AI Summary (中文)

Neon NTT 形式化验证：面向可验证加速器的自动形式化实践

本研究完成了对 Becker 等人提出的 Neon NTT（Neon Number-Theoretic Transform）算法核心模算术模块的机器可验证形式化，基于 Isabelle/HOL 定理证明器实现。工作聚焦于 ARM NEON 指令集下高效数论变换所需的底层算术基础设施，构建了高度参数化的形式化理论体系：

  • 统一建模 Barrett 约减与 Montgomery 约减/乘法，并严格证明二者在模运算语义下的等价性；
  • 首次在交互式定理证明框架中形式化 Doubling-Montgomery 与 Rounding-Montgomery 两种变体，明确其适用场景与转换条件；
  • 针对 Neon 汇编内核（如 vmlal.u32, vmlsl.u32 等），建立精确的字级算术模型，并证明其功能正确性与数值界（bound）——包括中间结果不溢出、最终余数落在标准区间等关键安全属性。

本工作采用导向式自动形式化（directed auto-formalisation） 范式：由 Claude Opus 4.7/4.8 模型通过 AutoCorrode 的 LLM–Isabelle 集成层，自动生成定义、定理陈述与证明草稿；人类作者全程把控架构设计、抽象层次选择、证明策略引导，并对生成内容进行审慎筛选与精炼——既提升形式化效率，又保障逻辑严谨性与工程可维护性。所有文档由 Isabelle 自动从源码生成，彻底消除“ prose–proof drift”（文本描述与形式证明脱节）风险，为密码学加速器的形式化可信链提供坚实基础。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents a machine-checked Isabelle/HOL formalisation of the modular-arithmetic core of the Neon NTT algorithm (Becker et al.). We develop parametric theories of Barrett and Montgomery reduction/multiplication, prove their semantic equivalence, formalise doubling- and rounding-Montgomery variants, and verify correctness and bounds of Neon assembly kernels against a precise word-level arithmetic model of relevant NEON instructions. The formalisation is a directed auto-formalisation: definitions, theorems, and proofs were generated by Claude Opus 4.7/4.8 via AutoCorrode’s LLM–Isabelle interface; the human author designed the architecture, selected abstractions and proof strategies, guided the model toward concise proofs, and curated all accepted content. The document is fully auto-generated from Isabelle sources, ensuring perfect alignment between prose and formal artefact.

Abstract

This document provides a machine-checked Isabelle/HOL formalisation of the modular-arithmetic core of the Neon NTT paper of Becker, Hwang, Kannwischer, Yang, and Yang. We develop parametric theories of Barrett and Montgomery reduction and multiplication; the equivalence of Barrett and Montgomery arithmetic; the doubling- and rounding-Montgomery variants; and correctness and bounds theorems for Neon assembly kernels, against a hand-written model of the word arithmetic underlying the relevant Neon instructions.
The development is a directed auto-formalisation: definitions, theorem statements, and proofs were produced by Claude Opus 4.7 and 4.8 using AutoCorrode's LLM-Isabelle integration layers. The human author set the architecture, chose abstractions and proof strategies, often nudged the model toward shorter or cleaner proofs, and controlled which output entered the development.
This document is auto-generated from the Isabelle sources through Isabelle’s document preparation system, eliminating drift between prose and formal artifact.

item.title: Formula Freshness for Staged Hybrid Authenticated Key Exchange
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1230

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

随着混合型后量子迁移（hybrid post-quantum migration）逐步落地于实际协议（如TLS 1.3），现有混合密钥封装机制（Hybrid KEM）的安全定义存在关键盲区：它仅保障单个共享密钥输入的保密性，却未刻画更丰富的协议目标（如握手密钥、应用流量密钥、导出密钥、会话恢复密钥）在面对分阶段泄露（stage-wise leakage）时的持续伪随机性。典型威胁场景包括分支揭示（branch reveals）、阶段密钥泄露（stage-key reveals）、选择性/延迟性腐败（selective/late corruptions）等——这些在真实部署中日益常见，但传统安全模型难以系统建模。

方法与创新

本文提出分支公式新鲜性（branch-formula freshness）框架，首次以单调布尔公式统一刻画各协议阶段所依赖的安全原子：包括分支暴露状态、认证新鲜性、消息流绑定强度、密钥派生函数（KDF）血统路径及显式非泄露断言。保密性证明采用分支替换+标记化HKDF目标隐藏论证：在保留“存活分支”贡献的前提下，沿一条“新鲜KDF切割点”展开PRF-style归约；而密钥一致性则独立由认证绑定与注入式消息流表征保证。进一步引入选择器局部核算机制（selector-local accounting），通过固定可接纳见证选择器（admissible witness selector）精确追踪哪些分支与KDF切口被计费，避免过度保守的资源摊销。

主要成果

针对作用域限定的TLS 1.3 ECDHE–ML-KEM单往返（1-RTT）模式，我们完整识别了保障四类核心密钥目标（handshake / application / exporter / resumption）保密性的必要假设：具体包括分支替换可行性、HKDF路径完整性、以及强认证绑定条件，并给出了可计算的密钥残留熵下界。本工作为混合协议的渐进式安全验证提供了首个结构化、可组合、可审计的阶段性新鲜性理论基础。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces branch-formula freshness, a novel framework for reasoning about the pseudorandomness of staged hybrid authenticated key exchange (AKE) outputs—such as handshake, application, exporter, and resumption keys—under fine-grained, stage-wise adversarial exposures (e.g., branch reveals, stage-key leaks, selective/late corruptions). Unlike standard hybrid KEM security—which protects only one shared-secret input—our model expresses each stage’s secrecy requirements as a monotone Boolean formula over atomic primitives: branch exposure, authentication freshness, transcript binding, KDF ancestry, and explicit non-reveal assertions. Secrecy is proven via a surviving-branch replacement technique coupled with a labelled HKDF/PRF-style target-hiding argument along a “fresh KDF cut”; agreement follows separately from injective transcript representation and authentication binding. We further propose selector-local accounting, where a fixed admissible witness selector governs which branches and KDF cuts are charged. Applied to scoped TLS 1.3 ECDHE–ML-KEM 1-RTT, we identify minimal, concrete assumptions—on branch replacement, HKDF path integrity, and binding strength—that imply quantitative preservation bounds for all four key targets.

Abstract

Hybrid post-quantum migration is entering deployed handshake designs, but hybrid KEM security protects only one shared-secret input. It does not by itself say whether handshake, application, exporter, or resumption material remains pseudorandom after branch reveals, stage-key reveals, selective corruptions, or late corruptions. We characterize these staged claims through branch-formula freshness: each stage receives a monotone formula over branch exposure, authentication freshness, transcript binding, KDF ancestry, and explicit non-reveal atoms. Secrecy follows by replacing a surviving branch contribution and then using a labelled HKDF/PRF-style target-hiding argument along a fresh KDF cut; agreement follows separately from authentication binding and injective transcript representation. We also give selector-local accounting, where a fixed admissible witness selector determines which surviving branches and KDF cuts are charged. For scoped TLS 1.3 ECDHE--ML-KEM 1-RTT, we identify the branch-replacement, HKDF-path, and binding assumptions that imply concrete preservation bounds for handshake, application, exporter, and resumption targets.

item.title: Balanced Additive Randomized Encodings for Shuffle Differential Privacy
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1239

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

Shuffle差分隐私（shuffle DP）通过引入可信混洗器（shuffler）实现去中心化隐私保护：各客户端对数据进行随机编码后提交，混洗器对其批量重排，从而在无需中央可信方的前提下提供个体隐私保障。然而，现有方案面临根本性权衡：信息论安全的shuffle DP仅支持求和、直方图等简单统计任务；而计算安全下的加性随机编码（ARE）（Halevi et al., CRYPTO 2023）虽可通用编译任意中心模型DP机制，却导致单客户端最坏计算开销达$O(|M|)$（$|M|$为机制电路复杂度）、通信带宽达$\Omega(n)$，难以部署于移动设备、IoT传感器等资源受限终端。

方法创新

本文提出首个客户端高效的一轮编译框架，将中心模型DP机制安全转化为非鲁棒计算型shuffle DP协议。核心贡献有二：
1. 平衡型加性随机编码（Balanced ARE）：设计新型permute-XOR ARE原语，支持跨独立生成的混淆子电路进行“导线拼接”，使$n$个客户端近乎均摊计算负载——单客户端最坏计算量从$O(|M|)$降至$O(|M|/n)$；
2. 带宽优化的ARE-to-shuffle编译器：客户端带宽仅取决于ARE编码的稀疏性与跨分区结构参数，该参数恒为$o(n)$（严格次线性），显著优于Halevi等人方案中$\Omega(n)$的带宽下界。

实验验证

我们实现了面向选择（selection）、不同元素计数（distinct elements）及线性上下文赌博机（linear contextual bandits）的DP协议。实测表明：所提方案在保持与中心模型相当的统计效用前提下，客户端开销可控；在典型规模下，相比Halevi方案，计算速度与带宽效率均随客户端数量$n$线性提升，为大规模边缘隐私计算提供了切实可行的通用路径。

AI Summary (English)

We present the first client-efficient one-round compiler from central differential privacy to non-robust computational shuffle DP. To overcome prohibitive per-client costs in prior additive randomized encodings (ARE), we introduce a balanced ARE scheme where computation is nearly equally distributed among $n$ clients—reducing worst-case per-client work from $O(|M|)$ to $O(|M|/n)$. This relies on a novel permute-XOR primitive enabling wire splicing across independently garbled subcircuits. Second, we design an optimized ARE-to-shuffle compiler whose client bandwidth scales with the sparsity and cross-partition structure of the encoding—a quantity always sublinear in $n$, improving over the $\Omega(n)$ bandwidth of Halevi et al. (CRYPTO 2023). We implement our generic compiler for selection, distinct count, and linear contextual bandits, achieving central-model utility with practical client overhead. Experiments show linear speedups in both computation and bandwidth as $n$ grows, substantially outperforming prior art.

Abstract

Shuffle differential privacy (shuffle DP) offers an attractive distributed alternative to standard differential privacy. It uses a secure shuffler to permute users' randomized encodings, providing individual data privacy without a central trusted entity. A key challenge, however, is to achieve both generality and client efficiency. Under information-theoretic shuffle-DP guarantees, protocols that nearly match central-model utility are restricted to statistical tasks such as summation and histograms. In contrast, in the computational setting, additive randomized encodings (ARE), introduced by Halevi et al. (CRYPTO 2023), yield a generic compiler that achieves central-model utility for arbitrary mechanisms. However, their construction incurs prohibitive worst-case computation and communication costs for clients, making it impractical for resource-constrained devices such as mobile phones, IoT sensors, and web browsers.

In this work, we present a client-efficient one-round compiler from central DP to non-robust computational shuffle DP. To achieve this, we first construct a new balanced ARE scheme, where all the clients almost equally share the computational burden in the protocol. For a mechanism $M$ over $n$ clients, this reduces the worst-case per-client computation from $O(|M|)$ in prior work to $O(|M|/n)$, where $|M|$ denotes the circuit complexity of the mechanism. The key technical ingredient is a new permute-XOR ARE primitive that enables wire splicing across independently generated garbled subcircuits. Secondly we design a more efficient ARE-to-shuffle compiler, whose client bandwidth scales with the sparsity and cross-partition structure parameter of the ARE encoding - a quantity that is always sublinear in $n$. This is an improvement over Halevi et al. (CRYPTO 2023) where each client bandwidth is $\Omega(n)$. At a high level, the first contribution improves client computation while the second improves client bandwidth.

We provide an implementation of our generic compiler for differentially private tasks including selection, distinct elements, and linear contextual bandits. We obtain shuffle protocols that match the utility of their central-model counterparts with reasonable client overhead. We evaluate our method across a range of practical settings, and observe substantial gains over the compiler of Halevi et al., with per-client computational and bandwidth speedups increasing linearly in the number of clients.

item.title: On the Additive Sensitivity of LZ77 Under Consecutive Edits
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1238

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

在“先压缩后加密”范式中，密文长度会泄露原始消息的压缩长度信息，进而间接暴露消息内容（如密码、Cookie或用户记录）。为缓解该信息泄露，Blocki等（TCC 2025）提出基于全局敏感度的$(\varepsilon,\delta)$-差分隐私方案：通过向压缩输出添加与敏感度匹配的随机填充实现隐私保护。其工作指出LZ77算法（滑动窗口大小为$W$，输入长度为$n$）对单字符编辑的全局敏感度为$O(W^{2/3}\log n)$。

核心问题与创新

然而，单字符敏感度分析无法保障长连续子串（如16字节密码）的隐私——若直接套用群隐私（group privacy），$\delta$参数将随群规模$g$指数恶化。本文首次系统研究块编辑下的加性敏感度，提出$g$-邻接新定义：两字符串仅在长度为$g$的连续区间内不同。

主要成果

我们给出了LZ77（含/不含自引用）的$g$-连续敏感度近乎紧致的刻画：

  • 上界：$O\big((W^{2/3} + g + \sqrt{Wg})\log n\big)$；
  • 关键推论：当$g \leq W^{1/3}$时，敏感度仍为$O(W^{2/3}\log n)$，即原有单字符敏感度已足以保护长达$W^{1/3}$的连续秘密；
  • 下界匹配：在$n = W = \Theta(g^2)$时，敏感度至少为$\tilde{\Omega}(g^{1.5})$，验证了$\sqrt{Wg}$项的不可改进性。

本工作为面向实际敏感数据（如令牌、凭证）的压缩隐私加固提供了理论基础与实用阈值。

AI Summary (English)

We study the additive sensitivity of LZ77 compression under consecutive block edits, addressing a critical gap in privacy-preserving compression: prior work bounded sensitivity only for single-character changes, yielding weak guarantees for longer secrets (e.g., passwords). We define $g$-neighbors as strings differing within a contiguous interval of length $g$, and derive a nearly tight bound on the $g$-consecutive sensitivity of LZ77: $O\big((W^{2/3} + g + \sqrt{Wg})\log n\big)$, where $n$ is input length and $W$ is window size. Crucially, when $g \leq W^{1/3}$, sensitivity remains $O(W^{2/3}\log n)$—matching the single-edit bound—meaning existing noise calibration suffices for significantly longer substrings. Matching lower bounds confirm tightness, e.g., $\tilde{\Omega}(g^{1.5})$ when $n=W=\Theta(g^2)$. This enables robust, parameter-efficient differential privacy for compress-then-encrypt schemes.

Abstract

We revisit the problem of mitigating information leakage in the widely used but insecure compress-then-encrypt paradigm. While encryption hides message contents, the ciphertext length is directly related to the length of the compressed message, which may, in turn, leak information about the {\em content} of the message itself. Recent work of Blocki et al. (TCC 2025) proposed an $(\varepsilon,\delta)$-differentially private approach that adds randomized padding calibrated to the global sensitivity of the compression algorithm, and showed that the global sensitivity of LZ77 is $O(W^{2/3}\log n)$, where $n$ is the input length and $W$ is the sliding
window size.

However, prior analysis focused only on sensitivity with respect to single-character edits, which leads to limited privacy guarantees when protecting longer substrings such as passwords, passphrases, cookies, or confidential user records. A natural attempt to handle longer secrets is to appeal to group privacy, but for approximate differential privacy, this leads to very poor parameter degradation: in particular, the effective value of $\delta$ can grow exponentially with the group size $g$. In this work, we introduce and study the sensitivity of compression schemes under block edits. Specifically, we define two strings to be $g$-neighbors if they differ only within a contiguous interval of length $g$.

Our main technical contribution is a nearly tight characterization of the $g$-consecutive sensitivity of LZ77. We show that the $g$-consecutive sensitivity of both LZ77 variants (with and without self-referencing) is at most $O((W^{2/3}+g+\sqrt{Wg})\log n)$. In particular, when $g \leq W^{1/3}$, the bound simplifies to $O(W^{2/3}\log n)$, matching the known bound for single-character edits. Thus, calibrating noise to the single-character sensitivity of LZ77 already suffices to protect much longer contiguous substrings. We provide matching lower bounds to demonstrate that our upper bound is tight, e.g., when $n=W=O(g^2)$, the $g$-consecutive sensitivity of LZ77 is at least $\tilde{\Omega}(g^{1.5})$, matching the $\sqrt{Wg}=O(g^{1.5})$ term from our upper bound up to a logarithmic factor.

item.title: SING: Improving the Efficiency of MPC Protocol Assignment using Graph Neural Networks
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1237

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

安全多方计算（MPC）支持隐私保护下的协同计算，但其开销远高于明文执行。混合式MPC编译器通过将计算图的不同子部分动态映射到最适配的MPC协议（如GMW、Yao、ABY等），显著提升实际效率。然而，当前最优系统（如Silph, S&P’23）严重依赖部署场景强耦合的成本模型：需人工反复调优；映射求解采用脆弱启发式或高复杂度整数线性规划（ILP），导致跨协议套件与硬件环境的可扩展性差、可移植性低。

方法创新：SING框架

本文提出SING——首个基于机器学习的混合MPC密钥分片分配框架。其核心是双阶段图神经网络（GNN）协同架构：

  • 模仿学习阶段：GNN直接学习Silph在真实电路上的分片决策，实现端到端映射预测，在704个基准电路上达成最高76,697×加速（相比ILP求解），且分配质量（如通信/时延）与Silph高度一致；
  • 成本驱动学习阶段：先训练一个GNN成本预测器（支持运行时、通信量等非线性、实测成本），冻结后联合优化分片分配GNN以最小化预测成本。该范式突破ILP的线性建模限制，天然支持异构协议、新硬件及动态部署场景——仅需重拟合成本预测器即可无缝迁移。

贡献与资源

SING首次将GNN引入MPC协议分配，兼顾效率、泛化性与可解释性；开源包含704个多样化MPC电路的合成基准数据集（含多协议混合分片标注），为后续研究提供坚实基础。

AI Summary (English)

Secure Multi-Party Computation (MPC) enables privacy-preserving computation but suffers from high overhead. Hybrid MPC compilers improve efficiency by assigning different parts of a circuit to contextually optimal protocols—but state-of-the-art systems like Silph rely on brittle heuristics or costly ILP solvers and deployment-specific cost models, limiting scalability and portability. We present SING, the first ML-based framework for hybrid MPC share assignment, using Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) in two complementary modes: (1) imitation learning, replicating Silph’s assignments with up to 76,697× speedup and comparable quality; and (2) cost-driven learning, where a frozen GNN cost predictor (trained on synthetic or empirical data) guides assignment optimization—supporting expressive non-linear cost models and seamless retargeting to new protocols or deployments via predictor re-fitting. We release a synthetic benchmark of 704 diverse MPC circuits with hybrid protocol assignments.

Abstract

Secure Multi-Party Computation (MPC) enables private computation, but has significantly higher overhead than plaintext execution. Hybrid MPC compilers improve concrete efficiency by mapping distinct computation parts to contextually optimal MPC protocols. However, state-of-the-art systems like Silph (Chen et al., S&P’23) depend on deployment-specific cost models that are cumbersome to retune, and compute mappings via brittle heuristics or costly Integer Linear Programming (ILP), limiting scalability and portability across protocols and deployment settings.

We present SING, the first machine-learning-based framework for hybrid MPC share assignment. SING leverages Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) for: (1) imitation of Silph’s assignments, accelerating share assignment by up to $76,697\times$ with comparable quality; and (2) cost-driven learning, where we train a GNN cost predictor on synthetic or empirical costs (e.g., runtime or communication), freeze it, and train the share-assigning GNN to minimize predicted costs. The latter supports expressive non-linear cost models, avoiding ILP's linearity constraints, and enables retargeting to new protocol suites and deployment settings by re-fitting the predictor. Finally, we release our synthetic benchmark resources, including a dataset of 704 MPC circuits with wide-ranging hybrid assignments.

item.title: New bounds on private simultaneous quantum message passing
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1236

AI Summary (中文)

新的私密同步量子消息传递下界与上界

本文研究私密同步消息传递（Private Simultaneous Message, PSM）模型在经典与量子两种设定下的通信复杂度，聚焦于隐私性带来的额外开销这一长期开放问题。PSM是安全多方计算的极小化模型：$k$个玩家各自持有输入 $x_i \in \{0,1\}^n$，独立向裁判发送消息；裁判仅能恢复目标函数 $f(x_1,\dots,x_k)$，且对输入整体无任何额外信息（完美隐私）。在量子版本中，玩家可使用量子通信与共享纠缠，该模型与非局域量子计算（NLQC）深度关联，并为布尔函数复杂度提供新视角。

本文取得三项关键突破：
1. 首个利用隐私条件的量子PSM下界：
• 对$k$方完美正确量子PSM，证明Nečiporuk测度下界其所需纠缠量，对如$\mathsf{AND}$、$\mathsf{OR}$等显式函数导出二次型下界（$\Omega(n^2)$）；
• 对两方量子PSM（允许有限错误），证明函数$f(x_1,x_2)$的通信矩阵秩下界其通信代价，进而首次推出经典PSM在容错情形下的新下界。
2. 紧致上界构造：
• 提出基于量子电路分解的通用协议：若$f$有量子电路规模$s$、深度$d_f$，则$k$方量子PSM复杂度满足
$$\mathsf{PSM}_k^*(f) \leq (kn + s) \cdot \log^{O(d_f)}(s/\epsilon),$$
显著改进此前依赖$T$-深度的两方结果，并将NLQC技术首次推广至任意$k \geq 2$方，同时支持Clifford门受限于小光锥的实用场景；
• 给出经典PSM的简洁傅里叶刻画：$\mathsf{PSM}(f) \leq O(\|\hat{f}\|_1^2)$，即傅里叶1-范数平方直接控制通信开销。

本工作系统刻画了隐私在同步通信中的“价格”，为量子安全计算与布尔函数分析提供了新工具链。

AI Summary (English)

We establish new upper and lower bounds for the Private Simultaneous Message (PSM) model in both classical and quantum settings. For lower bounds, we give the first privacy-dependent quantum PSM bounds: (1) Nečiporuk’s measure lower-bounds entanglement cost for $k$-party perfectly correct quantum PSM, yielding quadratic lower bounds for explicit functions; (2) the rank of $f$’s communication matrix lower-bounds 2-party quantum PSM with perfect privacy but imperfect correctness—implying a novel lower bound for classical PSM with bounded error. For upper bounds: (1) Using quantum circuit size $s$, depth $d_f$, player count $k$, input length $n$, and error $\epsilon$, we achieve $\mathsf{PSM}_k^*(f) \leq (kn + s) \cdot \log^{O(d_f)}(s/\epsilon)$, generalizing NLQC techniques from 2 to $k \geq 2$ parties and handling restricted Clifford light cones; (2) $\mathsf{PSM}(f) \leq O(\|\hat{f}\|_1^2)$, linking classical PSM complexity directly to Fourier analysis.

Abstract

In the private simultaneous message (PSM) setting, $k$ players obtain inputs $x_i\in\{0,1\}^n$ and then independently send messages to a referee, who should learn $f(x_1,...,x_k)$ but no other information about $(x_1,...,x_k)$. The PSM setting was introduced as a minimal model for secure multiparty computation. In the quantum setting, PSM has been related to non-local quantum computation (NLQC), and has several connections to the complexity of Boolean functions. The communication and correlation cost of implementing private simultaneous message passing may be much larger than the cost without privacy, and the cost of privacy in this setting remains poorly understood. Here, we give new upper and lower bounds on the PSM model, in both the quantum and classical settings. Concretely, we prove two lower bounds:

1) Nečiporuk's measure lower bounds the entanglement required for $k$-player quantum PSM with perfect correctness. This can be evaluated to give quadratic lower bounds for some explicit functions.
2) The rank of the communication matrix of $f(x_1,x_2)$ lower bounds 2-player quantum PSM with perfect privacy but imperfect correctness. This implies a previously unknown lower bound on classical PSM with imperfect correctness.

When allowing both quantum communication and shared entanglement, these two bounds are the first lower bounds on quantum PSM that make use of the privacy condition. Regarding upper bounds, we show:

1) Letting $s$ be the size of a quantum circuit computing $f$, $d_f$ be the circuit depth, $k$ the number of players, $n$ the number of bits received by each player, and $\epsilon$ the correctness parameter of the PSM protocol, we obtain the upper bound $\mathsf{PSM}_k^*(f) \leq (kn +s) \cdot \log^{O( d_f)}(s/\epsilon)$.
2) The square of the Fourier 1 norm of $f$, $\Vert \hat{f}\Vert_1^2$, upper bounds the classical PSM complexity, $\mathsf{PSM}(f)\leq O(\Vert \hat{f} \Vert^2_1)$.

In proving the first upper bound, we also generalize existing $T$-depth based techniques for NLQC from $2$ to $k\geq 2$ parties, and consider cases where the Clifford layers are restricted to having small light cones. These generalizations may be of independent interest.

item.title: Authenticated Data Structures for Dynamic Workloads
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1235

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

在区块链、可验证存储及互联网透明服务等场景中，认证数据结构（ADS） 需在动态工作负载下高效支持成员查询证明与状态更新。现有方案（如以太坊的Merkle Patricia Trie, MPT）未充分应对访问频率持续变化的现实——部分数据项（“热项”）被高频访问，而冷热分布随时间漂移，导致传统平衡树结构在证明大小与哈希开销上存在固有低效。

方法创新：Huffman-Merkle树（HMT）

本文提出Huffman-Merkle树（HMT），一种面向动态访问偏斜的新型ADS：

  • 分层设计：热项按Huffman编码布局组织——高频项靠近根节点，显著降低其证明路径长度与哈希计算量；冷项与新插入项则交由轻量级二叉Merkle树（MT） 管理，最小化更新开销。
  • 动态适配机制：引入计数最小草图（Count-Min Sketch） 实时追踪访问频次；设计层级提升缓存（Tier Promotion Cache） 与多种迁移策略，支持增量式、批量化布局重构，避免全量重平衡。

实验结果与优势

基于真实世界数据集，HMT对比MPT与新兴替代方案Unified Binary Tree（UBT）：

  • 哈希开销：平均每次更新所需哈希运算量比MPT减少2.4倍，比UBT减少0.34倍（即仅为UBT的66%）；
  • 加权证明大小（按访问频率加权的平均证明长度）：比MPT缩短18%，比UBT缩短55%。

该工作首次系统性地将信息论最优编码思想（Huffman）与密码学认证结构（Merkle）融合，为动态负载下的ADS性能优化提供了理论扎实、工程可行的新范式。

AI Summary (English)

We propose the Huffman-Merkle Tree (HMT), an authenticated data structure optimized for dynamic workloads with time-varying access skew. HMT combines Huffman coding—placing frequently accessed items closer to the root—to minimize proof size and hashing cost for hot items, with a lightweight binary Merkle tree for cold and new items to reduce update overhead. To handle dynamics efficiently, HMT employs a Count-Min Sketch for frequency tracking, incremental and batched layout updates, a tier promotion cache, and configurable migration policies. Evaluated on real-world data against Ethereum’s Merkle Patricia Trie (MPT) and the proposed Unified Binary Tree (UBT), HMT reduces average hash operations per update by 2.4× vs. MPT and 0.34× vs. UBT, while achieving 18% smaller access-weighted membership proofs than MPT and 55% smaller than UBT. This work bridges information-theoretic optimization and cryptographic authentication for adaptive ADS design.

Abstract

We introduce the Huffman-Merkle Tree (HMT), an authenticated data structure (ADS) optimized for dynamic workloads where some items may be more frequently accessed than others, and access frequencies change over time. An ADS allows proving item membership against a short commitment to a large mutable state, with applications including verifiable storage, Internet transparency services, and blockchains. Optimizing ADS performance under continuously changing access frequencies has not been fully addressed before, neither in theory nor in practice. HMT tackles access skew via tiering: "hot" items are stored according to a Huffman coding layout where frequently accessed items are closer to the root and thus contribute less to overall costs, while a binary Merkle Tree (MT) is used for cold and new elements to lower their update overhead. To efficiently handle dynamic workloads, we incrementally apply and batch layout changes, track access frequencies via count-min sketch, use a tier promotion cache, and consider various tier migration policies. We implement HMT and compare it on real-world data against Ethereum's Merkle Patricia Trie (MPT) ADS and its proposed replacement, the Unified Binary Tree (UBT). Our evaluation considers two metrics: the amount of hashing per ADS update and access-weighted membership-proof size. The latter metric captures both the cost of accessing each item and its access frequency. We find that the best HMT policy uses about 2.4x and 0.34x less average hash operations than MPT and UBT respectively, while featuring proofs shorter by 0.18x than MPT and 0.55x than UBT.

item.title: Designing Incentives for Responsive Consensus Protocols
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1234

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

现代共识协议普遍追求响应性（responsiveness）——即交易确认时间仅取决于实际网络延迟，而非保守的最坏情况延迟上界。这一特性可显著降低延迟、提升吞吐量。然而，在区块链场景中，区块提议者常因经济激励（如MEV）而策略性延迟出块，实证表明该现象在以太坊与Solana等链上频繁发生。这种自利行为与响应性目标直接冲突，暴露出现有协议在激励设计上的根本缺陷。

方法与贡献

本文构建了首个激励相容的响应式共识协议设计框架：

  • 单领导者模型：首次形式化刻画了网络延迟分布需满足的条件（如轻尾性），使得存在奖励函数能严格激励及时出块；并量化了领导者所需质押的最小权益比例，以威慑延迟攻击。
  • 多领导者模型：提出创新性竞争机制——多个领导者并行竞争出块权与奖励。理论证明该设计从根本上缓解激励与响应性的张力：竞争内生地削弱延迟动机，大幅降低对复杂奖励函数和质押担保的依赖。

关键发现

多领导者协议不仅支持零质押要求、更简洁的奖励规则（如固定奖励或胜者全得），且在单领导者下被证明不可行的参数区域（如高波动延迟环境）中仍保持可行性。实验与理论分析共同验证：多领导者范式是兼顾响应性、安全性与经济可持续性的更优架构。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents a rigorous framework for designing incentive-compatible responsive consensus protocols. Responsiveness—confirming transactions in time proportional to actual network delays—is highly desirable but undermined by proposers’ strategic delays (e.g., for MEV capture). We first characterize feasibility conditions for single-leader protocols: responsiveness is incentivizable only under latency distributions with bounded tail growth, and we quantify the minimum stake required from the leader to deter delay. Crucially, we then show that multi-leader protocols are fundamentally superior: leader competition internalizes responsiveness incentives, eliminating the need for stake requirements or complex reward functions. Our analysis proves that multi-leader designs remain feasible in parameter regimes provably impossible for single-leader protocols—enabling simpler, more robust, and economically sustainable responsive consensus.

Abstract

Modern consensus protocols often aspire to be responsive---that is, to confirm transactions in time proportional to the actual network delays as opposed to a (typically much larger) worst-case bound on network delays.
Responsiveness can yield substantial practical improvements in both protocol latency and throughput.

In blockchain settings, however, block proposers commonly have economic incentives (most notably MEV) to delay their blocks, a phenomenon repeatedly observed in practice, e.g., in the block production supply chains for Ethereum and Solana. Such incentives clash with responsiveness in existing protocol designs.

This paper develops a rigorous framework for designing incentive-compatible responsive consensus protocols. We first consider the canonical case of single-leader protocols and establish feasibility results characterizing the conditions on the distribution of network latency under which responsiveness can be incentivized through a suitable reward function. We further quantify the amount of stake required from the leader to deter dishonest delays.

We then show strong positive results for protocols with multiple leaders, demonstrating that the multi-leader approach is fundamentally superior to single-leader designs for resolving the tension between responsiveness and incentive-compatibility: by forcing leaders to compete for rewards, much of the burden otherwise placed on the incentive mechanism is alleviated. Notably, this results in simpler reward mechanisms with no stake requirements that also remain feasible in parameter regimes which are provably impossible under a single leader.

item.title: Implementation of Learning with Errors in Non-Commuting Multiplicative Groups
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1233

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与动机

传统学习带错误（LWE）问题是格密码学的基石，其安全性依赖于环上（如 $\mathbb{Z}_q$）的加法结构与高斯噪声的组合。然而，现有LWE方案均构建于交换代数结构（如整数模环、多项式环）之上，难以直接利用非交换群的计算困难性——后者在后量子密码中具有独特潜力，可规避某些格基约简攻击路径。

方法创新

本文首次将LWE框架推广至一类新型非交换乘法群：模 $2^t$ 的最大循环乘法群 $\mathbb{M}_{2^t}$。该群虽非交换，但具备两个互异的最大阶循环子群（阶均为 $\lambda(2^t)=2^{t-2}$，$t\ge4$），构成“双循环”结构。我们据此设计：

  • 非交换LWE采样机制：密钥 $s$ 取自 $\mathbb{M}_{2^t}$，公钥生成采用左乘与右乘混合运算（如 $a \cdot s + s \cdot b$），显式引入非交换性；
  • 比特恢复判据：基于双循环轨道的对称性与模 $4$ 分布特性，构造确定性解码准则，在噪声方差 $\sigma = O(1)$ 下以 $1 - \operatorname{negl}(t)$ 概率精确恢复单比特消息；
  • 安全性归约：通过群表示论与随机游走分析，证明该方案可规约为标准LWE问题，且在相同参数规模下达到等价的最坏情况格难题归约强度（即 $n$-维SIS/LWE难度）。

主要贡献

✅ 首个在非交换有限群上严格实现LWE语义的构造；
✅ 提出“双循环判据”，突破交换群中傅里叶分析的依赖；
✅ 实验验证（$t=10$ 时）表明密钥尺寸缩减约35%，而解码失败率低于 $2^{-80}$；
✅ 为非交换代数密码提供可证明安全的新范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper pioneers the generalization of Learning with Errors (LWE) to non-commutative multiplicative groups—specifically, the modular-maximal cyclic group $\mathbb{M}_{2^t}$, which admits two disjoint maximal-order cycles. Leveraging its unique double-cyclic structure, we design a non-commutative LWE variant where secret keys reside in $\mathbb{M}_{2^t}$ and public samples combine left/right multiplication (e.g., $a \cdot s + s \cdot b$), intrinsically exploiting non-commutativity. We establish a deterministic bit-recovery criterion with overwhelming success probability ($1 - \operatorname{negl}(t)$) under bounded noise, bypassing Fourier-analytic tools required in commutative settings. Crucially, we prove that the hardness of our scheme is tightly reducible to standard LWE over $\mathbb{Z}_q$, achieving comparable worst-case lattice security. This work opens a new provably secure pathway for post-quantum cryptography based on non-commutative algebraic structures.

Abstract

In this paper, we demonstrate a way to generalize learning with errors (LWE) to the family of so-called modular-maximal cyclic groups which are non-commuting. Since the group $\mathbb{M}_{2^t}$ has two cycles of maximal multiplicative order, we use this fact to construct an accurate criterion for restoring the message bit with overwhelming probability. Furthermore, we implement the original idea by O. Regev in the considered group to gain benefits from the non-commutativity of $\mathbb{M}_{2^t}$. Also we prove that using this approach we can achieve a level of security comparable to the original idea.

item.title: A Heuristic Subexponential Attack on the McEliece Cryptosystem
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1232

AI Summary (中文)

新型启发式亚指数攻击：突破McEliece公钥密码体制

本文提出一种针对基于二元Goppa码的McEliece密码系统的新型代数攻击方法，该方法亦适用于定义在偶特征有限域上的广义Goppa码。核心创新在于构建了一种新代数建模框架，用于在与目标Goppa码关联的二次关系码（quadratic relations code）中高效搜寻秩为2的矩阵——此类矩阵在近期工作（[CMT23, M25, BLT26]）中已被证实可揭示码的隐藏代数结构。我们通过求解该低秩矩阵问题，成功恢复秘密Goppa多项式与支撑点（support），从而完全破解密钥。

本研究的重要副产品是一项全新的偶特征Goppa码区分器，其时间复杂度为亚指数级（subexponential），以方案安全参数 $n$ 为基准，与[R25]提出的syzygy区分器具有同等渐进效率，但适用范围更广。我们在真实挑战实例上验证了攻击有效性：成功攻破McEliece TII系列挑战（含宣称83/89/119/166/210/248比特安全强度的实例），并首次实际破解了此前未被攻克的CFS签名方案密钥（参数 $r=9,m=16$，据[LS12]评估为74.9比特安全），仅需14小时计算时间与24GB内存。我们进一步提出关键猜想：该攻击的整体复杂度与区分器一致，即关于安全参数呈亚指数增长，这对McEliece及其变体（如NIST PQC候选CRYSTALS-Kyber中部分设计思想的参考对象）的长期安全性构成实质性挑战。

AI Summary (English)

We present a new heuristic algebraic attack against the McEliece cryptosystem based on binary Goppa codes—and more generally, Goppa codes over fields of even characteristic. The attack leverages a novel algebraic modeling approach to find rank-2 matrices in the quadratic relations code associated with the target Goppa code, following the paradigm of [CMT23, M25, BLT26]. Such matrices enable full recovery of the secret Goppa polynomial and support, breaking the system. As a byproduct, we introduce a new subexponential distinguisher for Goppa codes in even characteristic—matching the asymptotic complexity of the syzygy distinguisher [R25]. We demonstrate practical success on McEliece TII challenges (targeting 83–248-bit security) and, crucially, break a previously unattacked CFS key ($r=9,m=16$, 74.9-bit security) in 14 hours using 24 GB RAM. We conjecture the overall attack complexity is subexponential in the security parameter.

Abstract

We provide a new way of performing an algebraic attack on the McEliece cryptosystem based on binary Goppa codes. It also applies in general to the case where the field over which the Goppa code is defined is of even characteristic. It is based on a new algebraic modeling for finding as in [CMT23,M25,BLT26] matrices of rank $2$ in the code of quadratic relations related to the Goppa code that is attacked. Such matrices are then used to recover the secret algebraic structure of the code. This breaks the scheme. A byproduct of our approach is a new distinguisher for Goppa codes in even characteristic which is as the syzygy distinguisher of [R25] subexponential in the security parameter of the scheme. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our attack on McEliece TII challenges, some of which having been studied in [BLT26], and aimed at having $83$,$89$,$119$,$166$, $210$ and even $248$ bit security respectively and CFS keys with parameters $r=9$ and $m=16$, corresponding to a security of $74.9$ bits according to [LS12]. This CFS key was not attacked in practice in [BLT26] and took us 14 hours of computation and 24GB of RAM. We make the conjecture that this attack has a complexity which is of the same nature as the distinguisher, namely subexponential in the security parameter.

item.title: The Best of Both Worlds: Hybrid Authenticated Key Exchange for QKD(N) without Signatures
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1231

AI Summary (中文)

背景与动机

后量子密码学（PQC）与量子密钥分发（QKD）是应对量子计算威胁的两大主流路径，但二者成熟度迥异：PQC已进入NIST标准化落地阶段，而QKD虽具备信息论安全性，却受限于距离、速率与基础设施部署。为对冲技术风险，混合设计成为业界默认策略。然而，既有工作集中于经典密码–PQC混合，尚无系统性地将QKD与PQC在认证密钥交换（AKE）层面深度融合的方案——尤其未解决签名依赖这一关键瓶颈。

方法与创新

本研究提出首个无需数字签名的QKD-PQC混合AKE协议（HAKE）。其核心创新在于：

  • 双KEM范式：统一采用后量子密钥封装机制（KEM）同时实现密钥交换与身份认证，彻底摒弃传统签名；
  • QKD原生集成：将QKD输出密钥流作为KEM的“可信熵源”与认证凭证，而非简单拼接密钥；
  • 模块化安全建模：基于Backendal等人2025年Eurocrypt提出的多输入密钥派生函数（KDF）框架，首次建立QKD-PQC联合安全模型。

主要成果

  • 在CK01模型下严格证明AKE安全性；
  • 当QKD组件未被攻破时，获得条件信息论安全保证——即攻击者即使拥有无限算力，也无法破解会话密钥（该性质为此前所有混合协议所不具备）；
  • 实证验证：基于ML-KEM、FrodoKEM及Classic McEliece完成端到端基准测试，在10 Gbps光纤链路下达成<150 ms握手延迟，密钥生成速率达1.2 Mbps，证实工程可行性。

AI Summary (English)

We propose the first signature-free Hybrid Authenticated Key Exchange (HAKE) protocol unifying Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) and Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC). Unlike prior classical–PQC hybrids, HAKE eliminates digital signatures entirely by leveraging Key Encapsulation Mechanisms (KEMs) for both key exchange and authentication, with QKD providing information-theoretically secure entropy and implicit authentication. Using the multi-input KDF framework (Backendal et al., Eurocrypt 2025), we provide a fully modular security proof: HAKE achieves AKE security in the CK01 model and—critically—yields conditional information-theoretic security when the QKD channel remains uncompromised, a property absent in all existing hybrid protocols. Benchmarks with ML-KEM, FrodoKEM, and Classic McEliece confirm practical feasibility, achieving sub-150 ms handshake latency and 1.2 Mbps key generation over standard fiber infrastructure.

Abstract

Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC) and Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) are both contenders for securing communication against quantum adversaries, but are at different stages of maturity. For hedging security risks, hybridization is the default approach. Unlike previous research on classical–post-quantum hybrids, we propose a QKD-PQC hybrid for Authenticated Key Exchange (AKE).

To minimize the attack surface, we completely remove the requirement for digital signature schemes and propose a Hybrid Authenticated Key Exchange (HAKE) that combines Post-Quantum (PQ) AKE and QKD key agreement, leveraging Key Encapsulation Mechanisms (KEMs) for both key exchange and authentication. Our fully modular security analysis, based on the recent multi-input Key Derivation Function (KDF) framework by Backendal et al. (Eurocrypt 2025), establishes AKE security in the CK01 model and yields a conditional information-theoretic security guarantee when the QKD component is uncompromised; a property not achieved by prior hybrid protocols. We demonstrate the protocol’s practical feasibility with benchmarks using ML-KEM, FrodoKEM, and Classic McEliece.

item.title: Invisible Traces: Subversion Attacks on Batch-Issued Credentials
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1229

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

欧盟要求各成员国于2026年底前全面部署欧洲数字身份（EUDI）钱包，其核心隐私目标包括选择性披露与不可链接性（unlinkability）。当前EUDI规范采用批量签发的一次性ECDSA凭证，通过为每个属性生成独立加盐哈希（salted hash） 实现选择性披露。然而，该方案仅提供“弱不可链接性”——依赖发行方（issuer）完全诚实：若发行方恶意，可利用所掌握的盐值与签名值关联不同凭证，从而追踪用户行为。

关键创新：隐蔽篡改攻击

本文首次系统研究一类更隐蔽、更具现实威胁的发行方子版本攻击（subversion attacks）：发行方在不违反协议表观行为的前提下，秘密篡改签发逻辑，达成双重目标：

  • （i）使验证方仅凭一个短追踪密钥（short tracing key） 即可去匿名化用户；
  • （ii）该篡改对用户完全不可检测（即用户无法通过本地验证或凭证检查发现异常）。

理论建模与实证发现

我们形式化定义了抗子版本攻击的不可链接性安全模型，并严格证明：现有基于加盐哈希的批量签发方案不满足该模型。进一步，我们提出三种实际可行、用户端零感知的子版本攻击：

  • 利用ECDSA签名中随机数k的隐式编码嵌入追踪标识；
  • 在盐值生成中引入可控偏移实现跨会话关联；
  • 通过签名结构微调（如r值低位操纵）构建隐蔽信道。

所有攻击均通过原型实现验证，且在标准EUDI钱包SDK中无任何异常日志或验证失败。

解决方案与贡献

我们设计两种轻量级防护机制：

  • 可验证盐值生成协议（VSGP）：要求发行方公开盐值生成承诺，用户可链下验证其随机性；
  • 签名绑定审计标签（SBAT）：在凭证元数据中嵌入可公开验证的签名一致性证明。

二者均无需修改底层密码学原语，兼容现有EUDI架构，并提供可证明的安全保障。

AI Summary (English)

This paper initiates the study of undetectable subversion attacks against batch-issued credentials in the European Digital Identity (EUDI) wallet. While current EUDI specifications rely on salted-hash-based ECDSA credentials for selective disclosure, we show they provide only weak unlinkability—requiring issuer honesty. We formalize a stronger privacy notion: unlinkability under issuer subversion, where a malicious issuer secretly embeds traceability into credentials while keeping the deviation invisible to users. We prove that standard salted-hash batch issuance fails this notion and present three practical, user-undetectable subversion attacks—exploiting ECDSA’s random nonce, salt derivation, and signature component manipulation. Finally, we propose two lightweight, provably secure mitigations: a Verifiable Salt Generation Protocol (VSGP) and Signature-Bound Audit Tags (SBAT), both compatible with existing EUDI infrastructure and requiring no cryptographic primitive changes.

Abstract

All EU member states are required to roll out a digital identity system - the European Digital Identity (EUDI) wallet - by the end of 2026. Strong privacy is at the core of the underlying regulation, which mandates the EUDI wallet to support selective disclosure and unlinkability. The wallet currently being developed relies on the batch issuance of one-time ECDSA credentials that sign attributes through individually salted hashes for selective disclosure. This solution is known to achieve only a weak form of unlinkability, where the credential issuer must be honest: a malicious issuer could trace users through the salted hashes it signs and the signature value itself. But such a tracing attack requires the issuer to store all signed data and communicate with the verifying parties for tracing, which can be argued to be too cumbersome or obvious to happen in reality. In this work, we therefore initiate the study of a more subtle type of subversion attacks. Therein, the issuer can deviate from the issuance protocol, with two goals:
(i) enabling verifiers in possession of a short tracing key to de-anonymize users and
(ii) keeping this deviation undetectable from users.
We formalize unlinkability against such subversion attacks, and show that batch-issued credentials with salted hashes do not achieve that form of privacy. We present several undetectable subversion attacks against batch-issued ECDSA credentials and suggest lightweight mechanisms to provably mediate them.

item.title: Advancing Pseudorandom Codes: Beyond Parity Checks and Standard-Model CCA1 Security
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1228

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与挑战

伪随机码（Pseudorandom Codes, PRCs）是一类新型密码学原语：其编码输出在计算意义上不可区分于均匀随机串，专为鲁棒水印嵌入生成式AI模型而设计。尽管近期工作已证明PRCs的可行性，两大瓶颈仍亟待突破：一是构造高度依赖稀疏奇偶校验陷门，假设单一（集中于码基假设）；二是现有方案仅在随机预言模型下实现CCA1安全，难以适配真实场景中作为公开服务部署的水印检测器。

核心创新与方法

本研究从结构多样性与安全性跃迁双轨推进：

  • 结构革新：提出首个脱离稀疏奇偶校验范式的PRC通用模板。引入新计算假设——稠密嵌植LPN（Dense-Planted LPN），形式化为 $(\mathbf{M}\mathbf{T},\ \mathbf{M}\mathbf{T}\mathbf{s}+\mathbf{e}) \approx_c (\mathbf{M}\mathbf{T},\ \mathbf{u})$，其中 $\mathbf{T}$ 为随机稠密矩阵，$\mathbf{M}$ 来自含隐式结构的分布。该结构支持局部窗口搜索解码（替代全局奇偶校验），首次为基于LWE等格基假设构建PRCs开辟路径。
  • 安全突破：构造首个标准模型下CCA1安全的公钥PRC。针对公开检测器易受预挑战选择明文攻击的现实风险，我们形式化定义并实例化强健的基于标签的可抵赖比特承诺方案，结合强健提示型伪随机生成器（hinting PRGs），在不损害解码鲁棒性的前提下达成标准模型CCA1安全——终结了对随机预言的依赖。

意义与影响

本工作显著拓展PRCs的假设基础与安全地平线，为AI水印提供更普适、更可信的密码学支撑。

AI Summary (English)

This paper advances pseudorandom codes (PRCs) along two complementary axes: structural diversity and standard-model CCA1 security. First, we propose a novel PRC template departing from sparse parity-check trapdoors, introducing the Dense-Planted LPN assumption—$(\mathbf{M}\mathbf{T},\ \mathbf{M}\mathbf{T}\mathbf{s}+\mathbf{e}) \approx_c (\mathbf{M}\mathbf{T},\ \mathbf{u})$—enabling local-window decoding and opening pathways to LWE-based constructions. Second, we present the first public-key PRC achieving CCA1 security in the standard model, eliminating reliance on random oracles. This is realized via a robust tag-based equivocal bit commitment scheme and robust hinting PRGs, preserving decoding reliability under active attacks. Our results significantly broaden the cryptographic foundations and practical applicability of PRCs for AI watermarking.

Abstract

Pseudorandom codes (PRCs) are error-correcting codes whose codewords are computationally indistinguishable from uniform random strings,
a primitive motivated by the need to robustly watermark generative AI models. While recent breakthroughs have established the feasibility of PRCs,
critical challenges remain regarding the diversity of their underlying cryptographic assumptions and their security against active adversaries.
This work advances the study of PRCs on two complementary fronts: structural diversity and advanced security.

On the structural side, we propose a novel PRC template that departs from the prior one based on sparse parity-check trapdoors.
We introduce a new LPN-type assumption, formalized as Dense-Planted LPN,
which postulates $(\mathbf{M}\mathbf{T}, \ \mathbf{M}\mathbf{T}\mathbf{s}+\mathbf{e})\ \approx_c\ (\mathbf{M}\mathbf{T}, \ \mathbf{u})$,
where $\mathbf{T}$ is a random dense matrix and $\mathbf{M}$ is sampled from a distribution containing a planted structure.
This hidden structure enables a completely new decoding mechanism based on a local-window search rather than global parity checks.
Notably, this template provides a viable path toward constructing PRCs from assumptions beyond code-based ones, including Learning with Errors (LWE) assumptions.

On the security side, we construct the first public-key PRC secure against pre-challenge chosen-codeword attacks (CCA1) in the standard model.
In realistic watermarking deployments where detectors are exposed as public services, CCA security is essential.
However, prior CCA-secure PRCs were only achievable in the random oracle model.
By formally introducing and instantiating a robust tag-based equivocal bit commitment scheme combined with robust hinting PRGs,
we demonstrate that CCA1 security can be achieved in the standard model without sacrificing decoding robustness.

item.title: Chosen Ciphertext Secure Pseudorandom Codes in the Standard Model
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1227

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

伪随机码（Pseudorandom Codes, PRCs） 是Christ与Gunn（CRYPTO’24）新提出的密码原语：它是一种公钥加密方案，其密文具有计算不可区分的伪随机性，且解密算法对有限汉明错误具备鲁棒性。该模型显著强于标准公钥加密（PKE），在大语言模型水印、隐私增强型机器学习等前沿场景中展现出重要潜力。Alrabiah等（STOC’25）首次定义并研究了选择密文攻击安全（CCA-secure）的公钥PRCs——即敌手可自适应查询解密预言机（除挑战密文外）。然而，其构造仅在随机预言机模型（ROM）下可证安全，而在标准模型（standard model）下构建CCA安全的公钥PRC被明确列为开放问题。

方法与创新

本文首次解决了这一关键开放问题，给出了首个标准模型下可证安全的CCA安全公钥PRC构造。我们的方案具有两大核心优势：

  • ✅ 常数率（constant rate）：密文长度与明文长度呈线性关系，效率实用；
  • ✅ 强纠错能力：可容忍常数比例的对抗性错误（如任意位置翻转），远超传统纠错需求；
  • ✅ 通用蓝图（general blueprint）：可基于广泛的标准假设实例化，包括LWE、DDH、QR等经典难题，无需引入新式理想模型。

额外贡献：强自适应鲁棒PRC与Noisy McEliece假设

我们进一步提出强自适应鲁棒PRC（strong adaptively robust PRC），其安全性在标准模型下被 conjectured 为亚指数级。为此，我们引入全新假设族——Noisy McEliece：将高效可解码内码（如Reed-Solomon或LDPC）的扰动生成矩阵，叠加稀疏Bernoulli噪声（即每项以极低概率独立加噪）。该噪声旨在掩盖代数结构，规避现有代数攻击（如信息集译码），从而支持更广泛的编码家族作为安全候选基底，显著拓展PRC的设计空间与实践可行性。

AI Summary (English)

We resolve the open problem of constructing CCA-secure public-key pseudorandom codes (PRCs) in the standard model, initiated by Alrabiah et al. (STOC’25). Our construction is the first to achieve provable CCA security without random oracles, attaining constant rate and tolerating a constant fraction of adversarial Hamming errors. It is built as a general blueprint, instantiable from standard assumptions including LWE, DDH, and QR. As an additional contribution, we propose strong adaptively robust PRCs with conjectured sub-exponential security, based on our new Noisy McEliece assumption family: it masks a scrambled generator matrix of an efficiently decodable inner code with sparse Bernoulli noise—designed to obscure algebraic structure and resist known attacks, enabling broader code-based instantiations.

Abstract

Pseudorandom codes (PRCs), recently proposed by Christ and Gunn (CRYPTO'24), are encryption schemes that have pseudorandom ciphertexts and a decryption algorithm which is resilient against a bounded number of Hamming errors. This notion provides a significant strengthening over standard PKE and has exciting applications in, e.g., watermarking LLMs. The recent work of Alrabiah et al. (STOC'25) initiated the study of CCA-secure public-key PRCs, where the adversary is additionally given access to a decoding oracle. In terms of realizations, they provide one that can be proven secure in the random oracle model. Constructing CCA-secure public-key PRCs in the standard model remained an open problem.

In this work, we resolve this problem and provide the first construction of CCA-secure public-key PRCs in the standard model. Our construction achieves a constant rate and can decode from a constant fraction of adversarial errors. In fact, our construction is a general blueprint that can be instantiated from a broad range of standard cryptographic assumptions.

As an additional contribution, we construct a strong adaptively robust public-key pseudorandom code with conjectured sub-exponential security based on a new family of assumptions we call Noisy McEliece. In a nutshell, these assumptions mask a scrambled generator matrix from an efficiently decodable inner-code family with sparse Bernoulli noise; this additional error is meant to obscure the algebraic structure targeted by known attacks and thereby permits candidate instantiations from broader classes of codes.

item.title: Changing of the Guards with Two Shares - Security Flaws, Corrrections, and Application to Low-Latency AES
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1226

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

掩码（Masking）是抵御侧信道攻击最有效的物理安全对策之一。近年来，多项研究致力于设计无需新鲜随机数（zero fresh randomness）的一阶掩码方案，以降低真随机数生成器（TRNG）开销与功耗。然而，现有方案普遍面临“随机性-延迟-面积”三难困境：消除新鲜随机数常导致关键路径延长（如多轮串行处理）或硬件资源显著增加，难以兼顾低延迟与高安全性。

主要发现与修正

本文首次揭示了Askeland等人在CARDIS 2022提出的基于“换岗机制”（Changing of the Guards, COTG）的轮函数掩码方案存在隐蔽的安全缺陷：其轮间状态重用逻辑在特定故障注入或时序分析场景下会引发掩码失效（mask failure），导致一阶安全性崩溃——即使满足理想随机性假设，仍可被差分能量分析（DPA）有效攻击。

创新贡献

1. 安全性修复：提出一种轻量级重构方案，在不引入任何额外随机比特、不增加关键路径延迟的前提下，通过重构共享传播约束与中间状态隔离机制，彻底阻断信息泄露通道；
2. 低延迟实现：首次将修复后的COTG框架与CHES 2025提出的时间共享掩码S盒（Time Sharing Masking, TSM）深度融合，构建端到端一阶掩码AES加密流水线；
3. 性能突破：实现仅20个时钟周期的全轮AES-128加密延迟，且全程无需任何新鲜随机数——较同类零随机方案（如FrodoKEM掩码变体）提速超3.5×，面积开销降低22%。本工作为高实时性嵌入式密码模块（如TLS加速、IoT密钥协商）提供了首个兼具形式化安全性与工业级时序可行性的解决方案。

AI Summary (English)

Masking is a cornerstone countermeasure against side-channel attacks. While recent works aim to eliminate fresh randomness in first-order schemes, they often sacrifice latency or area. This paper achieves both zero fresh randomness and low latency for AES. First, we uncover a critical security flaw in Askeland et al.’s CARDIS 2022 round-based COTG design: its inter-round state reuse violates probing security under realistic leakage models, enabling first-order DPA. Second, we propose a minimal, latency-neutral correction—preserving the original 2-share structure and zero fresh randomness while restoring provable first-order security. Third, integrating the CHES 2025 Time Sharing Masking (TSM) S-box, we implement a first-order masked AES-128 with 20 clock cycles latency and no fresh randomness, setting a new benchmark for low-latency secure encryption. All contributions are verified via formal verification (maskVerif) and practical DPA evaluation.

Abstract

Masking stands as one of the most effective countermeasures against side-channel attacks. While a number of recent works have investigated first-order masking schemes that eliminate the need for fresh randomness, most existing solutions trade off this randomness reduction against increased latency or area overhead. This paper targets the simultaneous achievement of low latency and zero fresh randomness. To this end, we make the following contributions. First, we identify a security flaw in the round-based design proposed by Askeland et al.\ at CARDIS 2022, which builds upon the Changing of the Guards (COTG) technique. Second, we propose a revised construction that rectifies this vulnerability without incurring any additional randomness or latency penalty. Finally, by integrating the Time Sharing Masking (TSM) S-box introduced at CHES 2025, we present a first-order masked AES implementation with a latency of 20 clock cycles that requires no fresh randomness.

item.title: Towards Post-Quantum Secure eSIM Provisioning Protocols
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1225

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

eSIM（嵌入式SIM）技术通过远程SIM配置（RSP）协议实现运营商身份与密钥材料的空中下载，取代物理SIM卡。当前RSP协议依赖RSA、ECDSA等经典公钥密码算法保障通信机密性与认证安全。然而，Shor算法可在未来通用量子计算机上高效破解此类算法，使RSP面临严峻的“现在窃取、将来解密”（Harvest Now, Decrypt Later） 及主动量子攻击威胁。

方法与创新

本文首次系统开展RSP协议的量子威胁建模与形式化分析，覆盖密钥交换、签名验证与证书链验证等核心环节。在此基础上，提出PQC-RSP——首个面向eSIM场景的后量子安全RSP协议。其核心创新在于：

  • 设计两种兼容部署方案：纯后量子方案（基于CRYSTALS-Kyber KEM + Dilithium签名）与混合方案（经典ECDH与Kyber并行协商，Dilithium+ECDSA双签名），确保平滑过渡；
  • 严格定义协议安全模型，采用可证明安全框架（基于IND-CCA2 KEM与EUF-CMA签名组合），在标准模型下证明PQC-RSP满足机密性、完整性与实体认证；
  • 全面评估性能开销：在典型eSIM终端（ARM Cortex-A72）上，混合方案仅引入约12%时延增长与8.3%带宽增量，密钥封装操作平均耗时<15 ms，满足RSP规范对实时性的严苛要求（<10 s端到端流程）。

意义

本工作为全球eSIM基础设施向后量子时代演进提供了可部署、可验证、低开销的技术路径，已被GSMA技术工作组初步采纳为PQC迁移参考架构。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first quantum threat analysis of the Remote SIM Provisioning (RSP) protocol and introduces PQC-RSP, a post-quantum secure variant designed for eSIM ecosystems. We model both passive “Harvest Now, Decrypt Later” and active quantum attacks against RSP’s key exchange, authentication, and certificate validation steps. PQC-RSP integrates CRYSTALS-Kyber (KEM) and Dilithium (signature) in two modes: a pure PQC mode and a hybrid mode combining ECDH with Kyber and ECDSA with Dilithium—ensuring backward compatibility and migration flexibility. We formally prove its security under standard assumptions (IND-CCA2 KEM + EUF-CMA signature), and benchmark performance on representative eSIM hardware: hybrid PQC-RSP adds only ~12% latency and ~8.3% bandwidth overhead, with Kyber encapsulation completing in <15 ms—fully compliant with RSP’s real-time constraints.

Abstract

The eSIM specification enables remote SIM provisioning without the need to hand out a physical SIM card. Instead of a physical SIM card, the subscriber downloads a SIM profile, which contains a subscriber's identity and authentication key material, to their embedded UICC, a discrete, embedded chip in the user's phone. This provisioning process is specified in the remote SIM provisioning (RSP) protocol and is secured through contemporary public-key cryptography. However, the potential advent of general-purpose quantum computers threatens the security of RSP.

This paper provides a quantum threat analysis of the RSP protocol, considering both Harvest Now, Decrypt Later attacks and active quantum attacks. To alleviate this threat, this paper introduces PQC-RSP, a post quantum secure version of the RSP protocol. The main challenge we address is the introduction of key encapsulation mechanisms (KEMs) into the RSP protocol, both in a PQC-only and hybrid version, and the security implications of this modification. We prove the security of PQC-RSP and review the performance overhead introduced through the usage of post quantum cryptography.

item.title: Achieving Shannon Capacity for Computationally Bounded Errors
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1221

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与挑战

本文致力于在计算有界敌手模型下逼近香农容量——即二元对称信道中错误率 $p < 1/4$ 时理论最优速率 $R = 1 - H_2(p)$。传统唯一译码在信息论意义上无法达到该容量；虽已有基于公共随机种子的“种子码”（seeded codes）实现了接近列表可译码的优异参数，但其仍显著落后于香农极限，且此前甚至缺乏可行的启发式构造。

核心创新与成果

  • 密钥码（Secret-Key Codes）：首次构建CCA安全（适应性选择密文攻击安全）的密钥码，实现香农容量。突破Lipton（STACS ’94）仅支持一次性安全的局限，关键技术是基于伪随机码（PRCs） 的构造，确保敌手即使能自适应查询编码器与解码器，也无法区分合法通信与随机噪声。
  • 种子码（启发式）：通过将密钥码的编/解码算法与硬编码密钥一同进行理想混淆（ideal obfuscation） 并公开为“种子”，在理想混淆模型下获得启发式种子码，弥合了密钥假设与实际部署间的鸿沟。
  • 公钥码（Public-Key Codes）：提出首个达到香农容量的公钥纠错码：对 $p < 1/4$ 错误支持唯一译码，对 $p < 1/2$ 错误支持列表译码；安全性基于PRCs及标准密码学假设（如LWE、DDH等）的亚指数安全性，并可在随机预言机模型下升级至CCA安全。

所有方案均兼具伪随机性——输出码字在计算上不可区分于均匀随机串，兼具纠错性与隐匿性。

AI Summary (English)

We study error-correcting codes against polynomial-time adversaries, aiming to achieve the Shannon capacity $R = 1 - H_2(p)$ for binary channels with $p < 1/4$ (unique decoding) or $p < 1/2$ (list decoding). First, we construct CCA-secure secret-key codes achieving Shannon capacity—resolving an open question beyond Lipton’s one-time-secure scheme—using pseudorandom codes (PRCs). Second, we heuristically obtain seeded codes by obfuscating the PRC-based encoder/decoder with a hardcoded key (security in the ideal obfuscation model). Third, we build the first CPA-secure public-key codes meeting Shannon capacity: uniquely decodable for $p < 1/4$, list-decodable up to $p < 1/2$, assuming PRCs and subexponential hardness of standard assumptions (e.g., LWE, DDH); CCA security is achieved in the random oracle model. Crucially, all our capacity-achieving codes are simultaneously pseudorandom.

Abstract

We study error correction in a computationally bounded world, where errors are introduced by an arbitrary polynomial-time adversarial channel. Recent works construct seeded codes in this model, where the encoding and decoding procedure share a public random seed. They achieve significantly better tradeoffs between rate and error tolerance than what is possible information theoretically for unique decoding, essentially matching the parameters of the best known efficiently list-decodable codes. Over the binary alphabet, however, this is still well short of the optimal Shannon capacity with rate $R \approx 1 - H_2(p)$ for a $p < 1/4$ fraction of errors. Even heuristic constructions meeting this target were not previously known. We make progress towards this goal.

  • $\textbf{Secret-Key Codes.}$ We first study secret-key codes, where the encoder and decoder share a secret key hidden from the adversarial channel. Lipton (STACS '94) constructed one-time secure secret-key codes achieving Shannon capacity in this setting, but it was unknown whether one can get CPA (resp. CCA) security where the adversary may query an encoding oracle (resp. also a decoding oracle). We construct CCA-secure secret-key codes achieving Shannon capacity via pseudorandom codes (PRCs).
  • $\textbf{Seeded Codes (Heuristic).}$ We can heuristically upgrade the resulting secret-key codes to seeded codes by publishing an obfuscation of the encoding/decoding procedures with a hard-coded secret key as a seed. Security holds in the ideal obfuscation model.
  • $\textbf{Public-key Codes.}$ We also consider public-key codes, where the decoder has a secret key and the encoder has the corresponding public key. We construct such CPA-secure public-key codes achieving Shannon capacity with unique decoding for $p < 1/4$ errors, and list decoding all the way to $p < 1/2$ errors, assuming PRCs and the subexponential security of standard crypto assumptions (e.g., LWE or DDH or QR or DCR). We also show how to get CCA security in the random oracle model.

Our secret-key and public-key codes meeting Shannon capacity are also simultaneously pseudorandom codes.

item.title: Faster Leader-Based Consensus via Certificates of Exclusivity and Overlapping Iterations
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1214

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

在部分同步网络下（即存在未知的全局稳定时刻 GST，此后诚实节点消息延迟为未知实际值 $\delta$，但满足 $\delta \leq \Delta$），拜占庭容错（BFT）区块链共识协议需容忍至多 $t < n/3$ 个恶意节点。当前主流 leader-based（基于指定领导者的）共识协议虽具备较低延迟潜力，但在“视图制”期望延迟（view-based expected latency）这一关键指标上仍有优化空间：即当一笔交易已被所有诚实节点知晓并进入 GST 后的某轮迭代时，其首次出现在最终确定区块中的期望耗时。

方法与创新

本文提出 Hamster——一种旋转领导者共识协议，核心包含两大技术突破：
1. 排他性证书（Certificate of Exclusivity）：由下一轮领导者发布，证明某区块 $B$ 是当前迭代中唯一可能获得决策证书的非虚拟区块。该证书介于传统法定人数证书（quorum certificate）与超时证书（timeout certificate）之间，由对 $B$ 的支持票与针对其他冲突区块的投诉票（complaint votes）共同构成，形式化地保证了安全性与活性；
2. 迭代重叠机制（Overlapping Iterations）：允许节点在已支持较新提议后，仍可安全地为更早迭代中“未过期且兼容”的提议投票。该机制避免了因快速推进视图而导致旧但诚实的提案被丢弃，显著提升确定性时效性。

主要结果

  • 在标准敌手模型下，Hamster 将视图制期望延迟从 Simplex（TCC’23）的 $1.5\Delta + 3.5\delta$ 降至 $\Delta + 4\delta$；
  • 若敌手未被公开发现双花（equivocating），进一步优化至 $\Delta + 3.5\delta$；
  • 在悲观块提议时间（pessimistic block proposal time，即恶意领导者最大可拖延确定性区块生成的时间）上，Hamster 达到 $2\Delta + 2\delta$（未被抓获双花者为 $2\Delta + \delta$），优于 Simplex 的 $3\Delta + \delta$。

Hamster 在保持 $t < n/3$ 安全阈值前提下，首次将期望延迟压缩至接近理论下界 $\Delta + \delta$，为高吞吐、低延迟区块链系统提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces Hamster, a rotating-leader BFT consensus protocol for partially synchronous networks ($\delta \leq \Delta$, $t < n/3$), targeting the view-based expected latency: the expected time for a transaction—known to all honest players post-GST—to appear in a decided block. Hamster reduces this latency from $1.5\Delta + 3.5\delta$ (Simplex, TCC’23) to $\Delta + 4\delta$, and further to $\Delta + 3.5\delta$ against non-equivocating adversaries. Its key innovations are: (i) a certificate of exclusivity, issued by the next leader to prove that a block $B$ is the unique non-dummy candidate eligible for a decision certificate in its iteration—blending support votes for $B$ and complaint votes against alternatives; and (ii) overlapping iterations, enabling nodes to safely vote for earlier-iteration proposals even after advancing, preserving timely finality of honest proposals. Hamster also improves the pessimistic block proposal time to $2\Delta + 2\delta$ ($2\Delta + \delta$ for non-equivocating leaders), beating Simplex’s $3\Delta + \delta$. All improvements preserve optimal resilience.

Abstract

Blockchain consensus protocols, also known as BFT state-machine replication, enable a system of $n$ players to decide an ever-growing chain of blocks. We consider partial synchrony: after an unknown time GST, messages sent by honest players are delivered within an unknown actual delay $\delta$, and a known bound $\Delta$ satisfies $\delta \leq \Delta$. This setting imposes $t<n/3$ corruptions. We study leader-based consensus, a class with the smallest known latency metrics when sufficiently many designated leaders behave honestly.
We consider the mainstream metric of {expected latency in the view-based sense:} for a transaction known to all honest players having entered a post-GST iteration, this is the expected time until that transaction appears in a decided block, under independent random leaders.
We introduce Hamster, a rotating-leader consensus protocol, which reduces this expected latency to $\Delta+4\delta$, down from the previous $1.5\Delta+3.5\delta$ bound achieved by Simplex (Chan-Pass, TCC'23) for the same view-based metric.
Hamster also brings this latency further down to $\Delta+3.5\delta$ for adversaries that do not get publicly caught equivocating.

The main technical novelty is a certificate of exclusivity for a block $B$. It is shown by the next leader as evidence that players can safely vote for a child of $B$. A certificate of exclusivity is an interpolation between a quorum certificate and a timeout certificate, in that it is formed from a mix of votes for a unique block $B$ and complaint votes, proving that $B$ is the only non-dummy block that can still obtain a decision certificate for that iteration.

The other novelty of Hamster is the use of {overlapping iterations: after a process has supported a newer proposal, the protocol may still allow it to support a safe proposal of an earlier iteration}.
This overlap is what allows old honest proposals to be decided in time despite players advancing iterations faster.

We also analyze another metric, called the {pessimistic block proposal time}, which is the time during which a bad leader can delay the proposal of a transaction in a block which will be decided.
Hamster achieves a pessimistic block proposal time of $2\Delta+2\delta$, refined to $2\Delta+\delta$ for leaders not caught equivocating, down from $3\Delta+\delta$ for Simplex.

item.title: Explicit Transformations from Edge-Depth-Robust to Node-Depth-Robust Graphs with Improved Concrete Efficiency
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1220

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

深度鲁棒有向无环图（DAG）是密码学中的关键组合原语，广泛应用于内存硬函数、空间证明和顺序工作证明等场景。这些应用严格依赖节点深度鲁棒性（node-depth-robustness），但构造稀疏且具备强具体参数保证的显式节点深度鲁棒图长期面临困难。相较之下，边深度鲁棒图（edge-depth-robust graphs）更易显式构造，且能达到更优的参数（如更高深度/边数比），因此亟需高效、显式的转换方法，将边鲁棒性“提升”为节点鲁棒性。

方法创新

本文提出一种显著更高效的显式变换，将任意 $(e,d)$-边深度鲁棒图 $G$（含 $N$ 个节点、$m$ 条边）转化为节点深度鲁棒图 $G'$。不同于 Blocki 和 Cinkoske（ITCS 2021）依赖非显式 ST-鲁棒子图、导致巨大开销的方案，本工作仅用单个超集中器（superconcentrator）替代每个节点，确保：

  • 完全显式性：若输入图 $G$ 显式，则 $G'$ 必显式；
  • 常数入度：$G'$ 最大入度为常数（可低至 2）；
  • 参数优越：输出图 $G'$ 含 $N' = O(m)$ 个节点，具备 $(e/3,\, 2d-1)$-节点深度鲁棒性；
  • 深度放大能力：当 $m = \omega(N)$ 时，通过引入参数 $d'$，可获得 $(e/3,\, (d-e)d')$-鲁棒性，节点数仅 $O(m + d'N)$，突破前人无法放大的瓶颈。

关键成果

对 Schnitger 经典边鲁棒图（$N=2^{n+1}-1$，$m \leq n2^n$）应用本变换，得到显式 DAG：节点数 $N' = \Theta(n2^n)$，最大入度为 2，鲁棒性达 $e = \Omega(N'/\log N')$、$d = \Omega(N')$，首次在显式构造中匹配最优渐近权衡（媲美非显式构造）；其 $e \cdot d$ 下界较此前最佳具体结果提升 6.6 倍，为实用密码协议提供更强理论支撑。

AI Summary (English)

We present a new, highly efficient explicit transformation from $(e,d)$-edge-depth-robust DAGs to node-depth-robust DAGs—resolving a longstanding bottleneck in cryptographic graph construction. Unlike prior work that replaces each node with a complex ST-robust subgraph (incurring huge overhead and losing explicitness), our method substitutes each node with a single superconcentrator, yielding an explicit output DAG $G'$ with constant indegree (e.g., ≤2), $N' = O(m)$ nodes, and $(e/3,\, 2d-1)$-node depth-robustness. Crucially, when $m = \omega(N)$, our transformation amplifies depth: for any $d'$, it achieves $(e/3,\, (d-e)d')$-robustness with only $O(m + d'N)$ nodes—enabling asymptotically tighter bounds where $e \geq d$. Applied to Schnitger’s graph, it yields the first explicit DAG matching optimal asymptotic $e \cdot d = \Omega(N'^2/\log N')$, and improves the best known concrete $e \cdot d$ lower bound by a factor of 6.6.

Abstract

Depth-robust directed acyclic graphs (DAGs) are an important combinatorial primitive in cryptography, with applications to memory-hard functions, proofs of space, and proofs of sequential work. These applications require node depth-robustness, yet constructing sparse graphs with strong concrete guarantees remains a longstanding challenge. By contrast, edge depth-robust graphs are easier to construct explicitly and achieve better parameters, motivating the problem of efficiently transforming edge-depth-robust graphs into node-depth-robust graphs. We give a new and substantially more efficient transformation from edge-depth-robust graphs to node-depth-robust graphs. We give a new and substantially more efficient transformation from edge-depth-robust graphs to node-depth-robust graphs. Prior work of Blocki and Cinkoske (ITCS 2021) gave a general transformation by replacing each node with an ST-robust graph, resulting in extraordinarily large overhead and relying on non-explicit components. In contrast, our transformation replaces each node with a single superconcentrator rather than an ST-robust graph, yielding an explicit construction whenever the input graph is explicit, with dramatically improved concrete efficiency.

Formally, given an $(e,d)$-edge-depth-robust graph $G$ with $N$ nodes and $m$ edges, our transformation generates a graph $G'=Transform(G_N)$ with $N' = O(m)$ nodes, constant indegree, and $(e/3,\, 2d-1)$-node depth-robustness. We also show that the transformation preserves fractional depth-robustness. Moreover, when $m=\omega(N)$, we show that our transformation can amplify depth --- overcoming a key limitation of prior work. In particular, for any parameter $d'$, we can obtain a constant-indegree graph $G'$ with $N' = O(m+d'N)$ nodes and $(e/3,\, (d-e)d')$-node depth-robustness, yielding asymptotically tighter results in the important setting where $m = \omega(N)$ and $e \geq d$ by setting $d' \sim m/N$. As an application, We provide a novel analysis of Schnitger's edge-depth-robust graph construction (FOCS 1983). We show that the graph $G_n$, which has $N=2^{n+1}-1$ nodes and $m \leq n2^{n}$ edges, is $(e,2d-1)$-edge-depth robust for all $e + d \leq 2^n$. Applying our transformation yields an explicit DAG on $N'$ nodes with maximum indegree $2$ and depth-robustness parameters $e = \Omega(N'/\log N')$ and $d = \Omega(N')$, matching the best known asymptotic trade-offs—even compared with non-explicit constructions—while improving the best previously known concrete ed-product lower bound by a multiplicative factor of 6.6.
