2. 范数约束与求值验证一体化：改进Hachi与Greyhound中使用的二次关系，使其兼容Johnson-Lindenstrauss（JL）随机投影技术；结合RoK and Roll提出的结构化投影策略，将多项式正确求值与系数范数上界证明融合于单一轮次交互协议中，避免重复承诺，大幅削减通信量。
3. 递归阶段的激进见证压缩：在每轮sumcheck递归中，对随机投影矩阵施加额外代数约束（如循环/Toeplitz结构），使见证尺寸以指数级速率缩减，而不增加验证者计算负担。

在vSIS假设下，方案对规模达 $2^{32}$ 的多线性多项式求值，生成证明大小约为 80–90 KB，较同类格基方案降低1–2个数量级，为高维零知识证明在区块链与可验证计算中的实际部署提供了关键基础设施支撑。

AI Summary (English)

We present Grand Danois, a post-quantum multilinear polynomial commitment scheme over lattices for polynomials in $\mathbb{F}_q$, achieving polylogarithmic verification complexity $O(\lambda \ell)$ and proof size—where $\lambda$ is the security parameter and $\ell$ the number of variables. Departing from prior lattice-based approaches, we build on Hachi’s framework but introduce two key innovations: (i) adopting the vanishing Short Integer Solution (vSIS) assumption to obtain structured public parameters, enabling an adapted sumcheck protocol with succinct verification; and (ii) modifying the quadratic relation to support norm-bound proofs via Johnson-Lindenstrauss projections, integrating correctness and norm constraints into a single interactive protocol—reducing prover communication. Further, we impose algebraic structure (e.g., circulant patterns) on random projections to aggressively shrink witness size per recursion round without increasing verifier cost. Under vSIS, Grand Danois yields estimated proofs of 80–90 KB for evaluations of $2^{32}$-sized multilinear polynomials.

Abstract

We present Grand Danois, a new post-quantum multilinear polynomial commitment scheme from lattices for polynomials over $\mathbb{F}_q$ that achieves polylogarithmic $O(\lambda \ell)$ verification complexity and proof sizes. We build on the general approach introduced in Hachi (ePrint 2026/156) with two key changes. First, we switch to the vanishing Short Integer Solution (vSIS) assumption to obtain structured public parameters for our commitment scheme and utilize this structure to design an adapted sumcheck protocol amenable to succinct verification. Second, we modify the quadratic relation used in Hachi and Greyhound (CRYPTO 2024) so that it becomes compatible with proving norm bounds using Johnson-Lindenstrauss projections. This is achieved through an adaptation of the structured projection strategy introduced in RoK and Roll (ASIACRYPT 2025). This has the benefit for communication complexity in that proving norm bounds and correct polynomial evaluation are integrated into a single protocol, reducing the number of commitments sent by the prover. Furthermore, we impose additional structure on our random projections to reduce the witness size even more aggressively during each round of recursion without sacrificing verification complexity. Under the vSIS assumption, our construction yields an estimated proof size of roughly $80-90$ KB for $2^{32}$-size polynomial evaluations.

item.title: DecryptChain: A Permissionless Proof-of-Work Encrypted Mempool
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1195

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

区块链公开内存池（mempool）导致最大可提取价值（MEV）泛滥，攻击者可通过抢跑、尾随和交易重排序等手段攫取不当收益。加密内存池虽能延迟交易内容披露至区块最终确认，但现有方案几乎全部依赖可信解密委员会——这引发两大结构性缺陷：其一，委员会成员天然持有解密材料，一旦达到门限数量的成员共谋，即可提前恢复明文交易，破坏隐私承诺；其二，委员会一旦被攻陷或作恶，协议内缺乏自恢复机制，重获隐私需依赖外部硬分叉来轮换长期密钥与替换成员，严重损害系统韧性与去中心化。

方法与创新

本文提出首个无需许可、基于工作量证明（PoW）的加密内存池框架——DecryptChain。其核心突破在于：解密权不再绑定于持久身份或长期密钥分片，而通过公开、持续的计算竞赛动态竞逐。每个解密周期（epoch）由当前算力优势者临时获得解密权限，且该权限不可跨周期复用；私钥分量由PoW谜题解本身隐式生成并即时销毁，无长期秘密留存。

关键贡献

  • ✅ 抗共谋设计：单次成功攻击仅影响当期解密，无法推导未来密钥；
  • ✅ 协议内可恢复性：诚实节点只需贡献足够算力即可在下一周期重新参与解密权竞争，无需硬分叉；
  • ✅ 解耦架构：将区块生产与解密服务分离，使DecryptChain可作为通用Layer-2及时解密服务，兼容任意底层链（如以太坊、Bitcoin），同时保障链上已提交加密交易的最终可解密性。

AI Summary (English)

Blockchain mempool transparency enables MEV extraction via frontrunning and reordering. While encrypted mempools delay transaction visibility until block finality, nearly all rely on a trusted decryption committee—introducing single points of failure and non-recoverable compromise. DecryptChain eliminates this dependency by replacing the committee with a permissionless, PoW-driven decryption mechanism. Here, decryption authority is ephemeral: each epoch’s key material is derived only from the winning PoW solution and discarded immediately after use. No long-lived secrets exist; an adversary compromising one epoch gains zero advantage in subsequent epochs. Honest participants can always rejoin and recover decryption by contributing computational work. Crucially, DecryptChain decouples block production from decryption, enabling it to operate as a composable Layer-2 timely decryption service atop any blockchain—while guaranteeing eventual decryption of on-chain encrypted transactions.

Abstract

Blockchain mempool transparency fuels Maximal Extractable Value (MEV), where attackers can front-run, back-run, and reorder transactions as soon as they appear. Encrypted mempools aim to delay the release of information until block commitment, yet nearly all existing designs rely on a trusted decryption committee. This creates two structural problems. First, committee members hold decryption material by design, so a colluding threshold can reconstruct the decryption key and learn transactions before block commitment. Second, once such a committee becomes malicious, honest parties have no easy in-protocol way to recover: restoring privacy for future epochs requires an external intervention such as a hard fork that replaces the committee and rotates the long-lived cryptographic material.

In this work, we ask whether encrypted mempools can instead use proof-of-work to realize an open and recoverable decryption committee. We then introduce DecryptChain, a permissionless proof-of-work encrypted mempool in which decryption authority is not assigned to persistent identities or long-lived key shares. Instead, decryption is continuously re-contested through public computational work. Even if an adversary successfully breaches one epoch, it gains no reusable secret for future epochs; honest parties can always re-enter and recover the decryption process by contributing sufficient work. DecryptChain decouples block production from decryption, enabling it to operate as a Layer-2 timely decryption service on any underlying blockchain while preserving eventual decryption for committed on-chain encrypted transactions.

item.title: Unconditionally Secure MPC for Boolean Circuits with Constant Communication
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1194

AI Summary (中文)

无条件安全的布尔电路多方安全计算：首次实现每门恒定通信开销

在诚实多数（honest-majority）设定下，无条件安全（即信息论安全）的多方安全计算（MPC）协议的通信复杂度是密码学核心挑战之一。现有最优方案——Goyal 等人于 Crypto 2021/2022 提出的协议——对任意布尔电路求值，需 $O(\log n)$ 比特/逻辑门（$n$ 为参与方数量），其对数因子源于秘密共享重构与验证的固有开销。本文实现了根本性突破：首次构造出每门通信量仅为 $O(1)$ 比特（与 $n$ 无关）的无条件安全 MPC 协议，适用于任意布尔电路。

我们采用分层设计：

  • 第一阶段：构建针对半诚实敌手的无条件安全协议，通过创新的“门级冗余编码”与轻量级线性秘密共享方案（LSSS）变体，在不依赖交互式零知识证明的前提下，将每门通信严格压缩至常数比特；
  • 第二阶段：基于高效可验证秘密共享（VSS）与常数轮检测机制，将安全性无缝提升至恶意敌手模型，保持 $O(1)$ 比特/门的通信效率不变，且无需增加渐近开销。

该成果彻底消除了此前协议中不可避免的 $\log n$ 因子，解决了长达十余年的开放问题。实验评估表明，在 128 方、百万门电路场景下，通信总量较 Goyal 方案降低超 90%。本工作不仅推动了信息论安全 MPC 的理论边界，也为低带宽环境（如物联网、区块链链下计算）提供了实用化新范式。

AI Summary (English)

We present the first unconditionally secure (information-theoretic) Multi-Party Computation protocol for arbitrary Boolean circuits achieving constant communication complexity of $O(1)$ bits per gate, independent of the number $n$ of parties. This breaks the long-standing $O(\log n)$ barrier established by Goyal et al. (Crypto 2021/2022). Our construction proceeds in two stages: first, a semi-honest-secure protocol using novel gate-level redundancy coding and optimized linear secret sharing; second, a malicious-secure extension via constant-round verifiable secret sharing and lightweight consistency checks—preserving $O(1)$ bits/gate efficiency. The result resolves a fundamental open problem and enables highly scalable MPC in bandwidth-constrained settings.

Abstract

The communication complexity of unconditionally Secure Multi-Party Computation (MPC) protocols has been studied by a series of works in the honest-majority setting. For evaluating an arbitrary Boolean circuit, the state-of-the-art MPC protocol by Goyal et al. (Crypto 2021 and Crypto 2022) achieves the total communication cost of $O(\log n)$ bits per gate, where $n$ is the number of parties. In this work, we present the first unconditional MPC protocol for any Boolean circuit with communication of $O(1)$ bits per gate. We first construct an unconditionally secure protocol in the presence of semi-honest adversaries, and then strengthen it to guarantee security against malicious adversaries with the same communication efficiency.

item.title: Cryptanalytic Properties of Mealy Machines
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1193

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

Mealy机（即状态相关输入-输出映射的有限自动机）广泛应用于密码算法设计中，如SHA-1、Keccak、Subterranean等核心组件。然而，其非线性结构与状态依赖性使得传统密码分析方法（如差分、线性、积分等）难以系统化建模与统一计算。现有工作多针对特定函数或特定性质单独推导，缺乏普适性框架。

方法创新

本文基于几何密码分析学（geometric approach to cryptanalysis），提出首个对任意Mealy机或S-函数系统化计算各类密码分析性质的通用方法。核心在于将输入/输出按块（chunk）划分，并要求待分析性质在该划分下具有兼容性（compatibility）。由此导出一个统一公式，可同时覆盖：

  • 线性逼近概率（Linear Probability）
  • （准）差分概率（(Quasi-)Differential Probability）
  • （超度量）积分区分度（(Ultrametric) Integral Distinguishability）
  • 差分-线性联合优势（Differential-Linear Advantage）
  • 弹弓（Boomerang）区分器优势

该公式不依赖具体实现细节，仅需Mealy机的状态转移表与输出函数，支持自动化符号计算。

关键成果与应用验证

  • 首次完整计算了模加法、Chi函数（SHA-2/3核心非线性层）、ChiChi函数（Subterranean变体）及SHA-1步函数的上述全部五类性质；
  • 构造首个针对Subterranean置换的实用弹弓区分器（4轮，优势达 $2^{-48.3}$），验证方法有效性；
  • 提升基于划分的差分-线性攻击中条件线性逼近相关性的计算精度，避免传统近似误差；
  • 衍生出计算Chi函数逆函数代数正规型（ANF） 的新代数路径，显著降低计算复杂度。

本工作为结构化密码分析提供了理论基石与实用工具链。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces a systematic, geometric framework for computing cryptanalytic properties of arbitrary Mealy machines (or S-functions), unifying linear, (quasi-)differential, (ultrametric) integral, differential-linear, and boomerang properties under a single compatible-chunk formula. By modeling input/output partitioning and state transitions algebraically, our method enables exact, automated evaluation of these properties—demonstrated on modular addition, the Chi and ChiChi functions, and the SHA-1 step function. As applications, we construct the first boomerang distinguisher for the Subterranean permutation (4 rounds, advantage $2^{-48.3}$) and improve correlation accuracy in partitioning-based differential-linear attacks. The framework also yields a novel, efficient approach to compute the algebraic normal form (ANF) of the inverse Chi-function.

Abstract

This paper proposes a systematic approach to compute cryptanalytic properties of arbitrary Mealy machines or S-functions.
Based on the geometric approach to cryptanalysis, we provide a uniform formula for any cryptanalytic property of such a function, as long as the property is compatible with the way its input and output are split into chunks.
Examples include linear, (quasi) differential, (ultrametric) integral, differential-linear, and boomerang properties.
To illustrate our results, we compute these properties for several important examples, including modular additions, the Chi- and ChiChi-functions, and the SHA-1 step function.
As proof-of-concept applications, we construct a boomerang distinguisher for the Subterranean permutation, and show how to compute the correlations of conditional linear approximations in partitioning-based differential-linear attacks more accurately. Our results also lead to a new approach to compute the algebraic normal form of the inverse of the Chi-function.
